revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''鳥居原狐塚古墳'''（とりいばらきつねづかこふん）は、山梨県市川三郷町（旧[[東八代郡]][[三珠町]]）大塚にある[[古...'
revision.text:
'''鳥居原狐塚古墳'''（とりいばらきつねづかこふん）は、山梨県市川三郷町（旧[[東八代郡]][[三珠町]]）大塚にある[[古墳]]。
[[甲府盆地]]南部、市川三郷町域の北東端に位置。[[曽根丘陵]]西端の支丘上に立地する。標高は320m付近。曽根丘陵は東海地方の弥生文化や畿内の古墳文化が流入した地域で、旧三珠町域にも大塚古墳や伊勢塚古墳などが分布する。
開墾による破壊が激しいが、1984年（明治27年）の調査で[[石室]]が発見され、昭和初年には仁科義男による調査が行われている。内部主体は割石小口積みの[[竪穴式石室]]で、内部には朱が塗布されている。[[埴輪]]は確認されていない。1979年（昭和54年）に[[発掘調査]]が行われ、副葬品としてほう製[[内行花文鏡]]、舶載の[[神獣鏡]]（対置式赤烏元年紀年銘鏡）、滑石製[[臼玉]]、[[鉄刀]]、[[鉄剣]]、銅鈴、[[土師器]]片、[[須恵器]]破片などが出土している。
赤烏元年鏡は国指定重要文化財で、[[東京国立博物館]]に所蔵されている。直径12.5cmの[[紀年銘鏡]]で、大陸南部の呉の年号である赤烏元年（238年）の紀年銘をもつ。日本列島では弥生時代には大陸から[[威信材]]として青銅製品が移入されており、山梨県内でも[[貨泉]]や舶載鏡や出土している。大陸で[[朝鮮半島]]から[[遼東半島]]地域を支配していた[[公孫氏]]は[[魏]]と敵対する一方で[[呉]]と外交関係があったが、鏡に記されている赤鳥元年には魏に滅ぼされている。『[[魏史]]』倭人伝によれば、日本列島では翌年に[[邪馬台国]]の女王[[卑弥呼]]が魏に[[朝貢]]して[[親魏倭王]]の称号を与えられたといわれ、こうした東アジアの政治状況のなかで、紀年銘鏡は呉と[[倭国]]間の外交関係、あるいは公孫氏を介しての通交によりもたらされたと考えられている（宮野淳一による）。
{{DEFAULTSORT:とりいはらきつねつかこふん}} [[Category:山梨県の古墳]]

'''鳥居原狐塚古墳'''（とりいばらきつねづかこふん）は、[[山梨県]][[市川三郷町]]（旧[[東八代郡]][[三珠町]]）大塚にある[[古墳]]。
[[甲府盆地]]南部、市川三郷町域の北東端に位置。[[曽根丘陵]]西端の支丘上に立地する。標高は320m付近。曽根丘陵は東海地方の弥生文化や畿内の古墳文化が流入した地域で、旧三珠町域にも大塚古墳や伊勢塚古墳などが分布する。
開墾による破壊が激しいが、1984年（明治27年）の調査で[[石室]]が発見され、昭和初年には仁科義男による調査が行われている。内部主体は割石小口積みの[[竪穴式石室]]で、内部には朱が塗布されている。[[埴輪]]は確認されていない。1979年（昭和54年）に[[発掘調査]]が行われ、副葬品としてほう製[[内行花文鏡]]、舶載の[[神獣鏡]]（対置式赤烏元年紀年銘鏡）、滑石製[[臼玉]]、[[鉄刀]]、[[鉄剣]]、銅鈴、[[土師器]]片、[[須恵器]]破片などが出土している。
赤烏元年鏡は国指定重要文化財で、[[東京国立博物館]]に所蔵されている。直径12.5cmの[[紀年銘鏡]]で、大陸南部の呉の年号である赤烏元年（238年）の紀年銘をもつ。日本列島では弥生時代には大陸から[[威信材]]として青銅製品が移入されており、山梨県内でも[[貨泉]]や舶載鏡や出土している。大陸で[[朝鮮半島]]から[[遼東半島]]地域を支配していた[[公孫氏]]は[[魏]]と敵対する一方で[[呉]]と外交関係があったが、鏡に記されている赤鳥元年には魏に滅ぼされている。『[[魏史]]』倭人伝によれば、日本列島では翌年に[[邪馬台国]]の女王[[卑弥呼]]が魏に[[朝貢]]して[[親魏倭王]]の称号を与えられたといわれ、こうした東アジアの政治状況のなかで、紀年銘鏡は呉と[[倭国]]間の外交関係、あるいは公孫氏を介しての通交によりもたらされたと考えられている（宮野淳一による）。
{{DEFAULTSORT:とりいはらきつねつかこふん}} [[Category:山梨県の古墳]] [[Category:市川三郷町]]

'''鳥居原狐塚古墳'''（とりいばらきつねづかこふん）は、[[山梨県]][[市川三郷町]]（旧[[東八代郡]][[三珠町]]）大塚にある[[古墳]]。
[[甲府盆地]]南部、市川三郷町域の北東端に位置。[[曽根丘陵]]西端の支丘上に立地する。標高は320m付近。曽根丘陵は東海地方の弥生文化や畿内の古墳文化が流入した地域で、旧三珠町域にも大塚古墳や伊勢塚古墳などが分布する。
開墾による破壊が激しいが、1984年（明治27年）の調査で[[石室]]が発見され、昭和初年には仁科義男による調査が行われている。内部主体は割石小口積みの[[竪穴式石室]]で、内部には朱が塗布されている。[[埴輪]]は確認されていない。1979年（昭和54年）に[[発掘調査]]が行われ、副葬品としてほう製[[内行花文鏡]]、舶載の[[神獣鏡]]（対置式赤烏元年紀年銘鏡）、滑石製[[臼玉]]、[[鉄刀]]、[[鉄剣]]、銅鈴、[[土師器]]片、[[須恵器]]破片などが出土している。
赤烏元年鏡は国の重要文化財に指定され、[[一宮浅間神社]]に所蔵されている（[[東京国立博物館]]に寄託）。直径12.5cmの[[紀年銘鏡]]で、大陸南部の呉の年号である赤烏元年（238年）の紀年銘をもつ。日本列島では弥生時代には大陸から[[威信材]]として青銅製品が移入されており、山梨県内でも[[貨泉]]や舶載鏡や出土している。大陸で[[朝鮮半島]]から[[遼東半島]]地域を支配していた[[公孫氏]]は[[魏]]と敵対する一方で[[呉]]と外交関係があったが、鏡に記されている赤鳥元年には魏に滅ぼされている。『[[魏史]]』倭人伝によれば、日本列島では翌年に[[邪馬台国]]の女王[[卑弥呼]]が魏に[[朝貢]]して[[親魏倭王]]の称号を与えられたといわれ、こうした東アジアの政治状況のなかで、紀年銘鏡は呉と[[倭国]]間の外交関係、あるいは公孫氏を介しての通交によりもたらされたと考えられている（宮野淳一による）。
{{DEFAULTSORT:とりいはらきつねつかこふん}} [[Category:山梨県の古墳]] [[Category:市川三郷町]]

revision.comment: 適切な項目へリンク - 呉 (三国)

revision.comment: 『[[魏史]]』倭人伝 というリンクを一般的な『[[魏志倭人伝]]』に修正
revision.text:
'''鳥居原狐塚古墳'''（とりいばらきつねづかこふん）は、[[山梨県]][[市川三郷町]]（旧[[東八代郡]][[三珠町]]）大塚にある[[古墳]]。

'''鳥居原狐塚古墳'''（とりいばらきつねづかこふん）は、[[山梨県]][[市川三郷町]]（旧[[東八代郡]][[三珠町]]）大塚にある[[古墳]]。
== 概要 == [[甲府盆地]]南部、市川三郷町域の北東端に位置。[[曽根丘陵]]西端の支丘上に立地する。標高は320メートル付近。曽根丘陵は東海地方の弥生文化や畿内の古墳文化が流入した地域で、旧三珠町域にも大塚古墳や伊勢塚古墳などが分布する。
開墾による破壊が激しいが、[[1984年]]（[[明治]]27年）の調査で[[石室]]が発見され、[[昭和]]初年には仁科義男による調査が行われている。内部主体は割石小口積みの[[竪穴式石室]]で、内部には朱が塗布されている。[[埴輪]]は確認されていない。[[1979年]]（昭和54年）に[[発掘調査]]が行われ、副葬品としてほう製[[内行花文鏡]]、舶載の[[神獣鏡]]（対置式赤烏元年紀年銘鏡）、滑石製[[臼玉]]、[[鉄刀]]、[[鉄剣]]、銅鈴、[[土師器]]片、[[須恵器]]破片などが出土している。
赤烏元年鏡は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定され、一宮浅間神社（市川三郷町）に所蔵されている（[[東京国立博物館]]に寄託）。直径12.5センチメートルの[[紀年銘鏡]]で、大陸南部の[[呉 (三国)|呉]]の年号である[[赤烏]]元年（[[238年]]）の紀年銘をもつ。日本列島では弥生時代には大陸から[[威信材]]として青銅製品が移入されており、山梨県内でも[[貨泉]]や舶載鏡や出土している。大陸で[[朝鮮半島]]から[[遼東半島]]地域を支配していた[[公孫氏]]は[[魏 (三国)|魏]]と敵対する一方で呉と外交関係があったが、鏡に記されている赤鳥元年には魏に滅ぼされている。『[[魏志倭人伝]]』によれば、日本列島では翌年に[[邪馬台国]]の女王[[卑弥呼]]が魏に[[朝貢]]して[[親魏倭王]]の称号を与えられたといわれ、こうした東アジアの政治状況のなかで、紀年銘鏡は呉と[[倭国]]間の外交関係、あるいは公孫氏を介しての通交によりもたらされたと考えられている（宮野淳一による）。 <!--宮野淳一なる人物について注釈がほしいです。（著作があるならば挙げていただきたい）-->
{{DEFAULTSORT:とりいはらきつねつかこふん}} [[Category:山梨県の古墳]] [[Category:市川三郷町]]

開墾による破壊が激しいが、[[1984年]]（[[明治]]27年）の調査で[[石室]]が発見され、[[昭和]]初年には仁科義男による調査が行われている。内部主体は割石小口積みの[[竪穴式石室]]で、内部には朱が塗布されている。[[埴輪]]は確認されていない。[[1979年]]（昭和54年）に[[発掘調査]]が行われ、副葬品としてほう製[[内行花文鏡]]、舶載の[[神獣鏡]]（対置式赤烏元年紀年銘鏡）、滑石製[[臼玉]]、[[鉄刀]]、[[鉄剣]]、銅鈴、[[土師器]]片、[[須恵器]]破片などが出土している。

== 赤烏元年鏡 == 赤烏元年鏡は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定され、一宮浅間神社（市川三郷町）に所蔵されている（[[東京国立博物館]]に寄託）。直径12.5センチメートルの[[紀年銘鏡]]で、大陸南部の[[呉 (三国)|呉]]の年号である[[赤烏]]元年（[[238年]]）の紀年銘をもつ<ref>赤烏の年号を持つものとしては他に安倉高塚古墳（宝塚市）出土の画文帯神獣鏡がある（赤烏七年）</ref>。日本列島では弥生時代には大陸から[[威信材]]として青銅製品が移入されており、山梨県内でも[[貨泉]]や舶載鏡や出土している。大陸で[[朝鮮半島]]から[[遼東半島]]地域を支配していた[[公孫氏]]は[[魏 (三国)|魏]]と敵対する一方で呉と外交関係があったが、鏡に記されている赤烏元年には魏に滅ぼされている。『[[魏志倭人伝]]』によれば、日本列島では翌年に[[邪馬台国]]の女王[[卑弥呼]]が魏に[[朝貢]]して[[親魏倭王]]の称号を与えられたといわれ、こうした東アジアの政治状況のなかで、紀年銘鏡は呉と[[倭国]]間の外交関係、あるいは公孫氏を介しての通交によりもたらされたと考えられている（宮野淳一による）。 <!--宮野淳一なる人物について注釈がほしいです。（著作があるならば挙げていただきたい）-->

開墾による破壊が激しいが、[[1984年]]（[[明治]]27年）の調査で[[石室]]が発見され、[[昭和]]初年には仁科義男による調査が行われている。内部主体は割石小口積みの[[竪穴式石室]]で、内部には朱が塗布されている。[[埴輪]]は確認されていない。[[1979年]]（昭和54年）に[[発掘調査]]が行われ、副葬品としてほう製[[内行花文鏡]]、舶載の[[神獣鏡]]（対置式赤烏元年紀年銘鏡）、滑石製[[臼玉]]、[[鉄刀]]、[[鉄剣]]、銅鈴、[[土師器]]片、[[須恵器]]破片などが出土している。

== 概要 == [[甲府盆地]]南部、市川三郷町域の北東端に位置。[[曽根丘陵]]西端の支丘上に立地する。標高は320メートル付近。曽根丘陵は東海地方の弥生文化や畿内の古墳文化が流入した地域で、大塚古墳や伊勢塚古墳などが分布する。
開墾による破壊が激しいが、[[1894年]]（[[明治]]27年）の調査で[[石室]]が確認され、[[昭和]]初年には仁科義男による調査が行われている。内部主体は割石小口積みの[[竪穴式石室]]で、内部には朱が塗布されている。[[埴輪]]は確認されていない。[[1979年]]（昭和54年）に[[発掘調査]]が行われ、副葬品として仿製[[内行花文鏡]]、舶載の[[神獣鏡]]（対置式赤烏元年紀年銘鏡）、滑石製[[臼玉]]、[[鉄刀]]、[[鉄剣]]、銅鈴、[[土師器]]片、[[須恵器]]破片などが出土している。
== 赤烏元年鏡 == 赤烏元年鏡は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定され、[[一宮浅間神社]]（市川三郷町）に所蔵されている（[[東京国立博物館]]に寄託）。直径12.5センチメートルの紀年銘鏡で、大陸南部の[[呉 (三国)|呉]]の年号である[[赤烏]]元年（[[238年]]）の紀年銘をもつ<ref>赤烏の年号を持つものとしては他に安倉高塚古墳（宝塚市）出土の画文帯神獣鏡がある（赤烏七年）</ref>。日本列島では弥生時代には大陸から[[威信材]]として青銅製品が移入されており、山梨県内でも[[貨泉]]や舶載鏡や出土している。大陸で[[朝鮮半島]]から[[遼東半島]]地域を支配していた[[公孫氏]]は[[魏 (三国)|魏]]と敵対する一方で呉と外交関係があったが、鏡に記されている赤烏元年には魏に滅ぼされている。『[[魏志倭人伝]]』によれば、日本列島では翌年に[[邪馬台国]]の女王[[卑弥呼]]が魏に[[朝貢]]して[[親魏倭王]]の称号を与えられたといわれ、こうした東アジアの政治状況のなかで、紀年銘鏡は呉と[[倭国]]間の外交関係、あるいは公孫氏を介しての通交によりもたらされたと考えられている（宮野淳一による）。 <!--宮野淳一なる人物について注釈がほしいです。（著作があるならば挙げていただきたい）-->
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)]] * [[曽根丘陵]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:とりいはらきつねつかこふん}} [[Category:山梨県の古墳]] [[Category:市川三郷町の歴史]] [[Category:山梨県の重要文化財]] [[Category:円墳]]

倣製[[内行花文鏡]]
[[鉄刀]]
陶磁片ほか |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''鳥居原狐塚古墳'''（とりいばらきつねづかこふん）は、[[山梨県]][[市川三郷町]]大塚にある[[古墳]]。'''鳥居原古墳'''とも呼ばれる。鳥居原は同古墳所在地周辺の通称。 == 概要 == [[甲府盆地]]南部、市川三郷町域の北東端に位置。[[曽根丘陵]]西端の支丘上に立地する。[[標高]]は320[[メートル]]付近。曽根丘陵は[[東海地方]]の弥生文化や[[畿内]]の古墳文化が流入した地域で、大塚古墳や[[伊勢塚古墳]]などが分布する。 [[開墾]]による破壊が激しいが、[[1894年]]（[[明治]]27年）の調査で[[石室]]が確認され、[[昭和]]初年には仁科義男による調査が行われている。内部主体は割石小口積みの[[竪穴式石室]]で、内部には朱が塗布されている。[[埴輪]]は確認されていない。[[1979年]]（昭和54年）に[[発掘調査]]が行われ、[[副葬品]]として仿製[[内行花文鏡]]、舶載の[[神獣鏡]]（対置式[[赤烏]]元年紀年銘鏡）、[[滑石]]製[[臼玉]]、[[鉄刀]]、[[鉄剣]]、銅鈴、[[土師器]]片、[[須恵器]]破片などが出土している。 == 赤烏元年鏡 == 赤烏元年鏡は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定され、[[一宮浅間神社]]（市川三郷町）に所蔵されている（[[東京国立博物館]]に[[寄託]]）。直径12.5[[センチメートル]]の[[紀年銘鏡]]で、大陸南部の[[呉 (三国)|呉]]の年号である[[赤烏]]元年（[[238年]]）の紀年銘をもつ赤烏の年号を持つものとしては他に安倉高塚古墳（[[宝塚市]]）出土の画文帯[[神獣鏡]]がある（赤烏七年）。[[日本列島]]では[[弥生時代]]には大陸から[[威信材]]として[[青銅]]製品が移入されており、山梨県内でも{{関連仮リンク|貨泉|ref=日本の書道史#貨泉}}や舶載鏡や出土している。大陸で[[朝鮮半島]]から[[遼東半島]]地域を支配していた[[公孫氏]]は[[魏 (三国)|魏]]と敵対する一方で呉と外交関係があったが、鏡に記されている赤烏元年には魏に滅ぼされている。『[[魏志倭人伝]]』によれば、日本列島では翌年に[[邪馬台国]]の女王[[卑弥呼]]が魏に[[朝貢]]して[[親魏倭王]]の称号を与えられたといわれ、こうした[[東アジア]]の政治状況のなかで、紀年銘鏡は呉と[[倭国]]間の外交関係、あるいは公孫氏を介しての通交によりもたらされたと考えられている（宮野淳一による）。 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)]] * [[曽根丘陵]] {{デフォルトソート:とりいはらきつねつかこふん}} [[Category:山梨県の古墳]] [[Category:市川三郷町の歴史]] [[Category:山梨県の重要文化財]] [[Category:円墳]]

陶磁片ほか |史跡指定= |特記事項= }} '''鳥居原狐塚古墳'''（とりいばらきつねづかこふん）は、[[山梨県]][[市川三郷町]]大塚にある[[古墳]]。'''鳥居原古墳'''とも呼ばれる。鳥居原は同古墳所在地周辺の通称。 == 概要 == [[甲府盆地]]南部、市川三郷町域の北東端に位置。[[曽根丘陵]]西端の支丘上に立地する。[[標高]]は320[[メートル]]付近。曽根丘陵は[[東海地方]]の弥生文化や[[畿内]]の古墳文化が流入した地域で、大塚古墳や[[伊勢塚古墳]]などが分布する。 [[開墾]]による破壊が激しいが、[[1894年]]（[[明治]]27年）の調査で[[石室]]が確認され、[[昭和]]初年には仁科義男による調査が行われている。内部主体は割石小口積みの[[竪穴式石室]]で、内部には朱が塗布されている。[[埴輪]]は確認されていない。[[1979年]]（昭和54年）に[[発掘調査]]が行われ、[[副葬品]]として仿製[[内行花文鏡]]、舶載の[[神獣鏡]]（対置式[[赤烏]]元年紀年銘鏡）、[[滑石]]製[[臼玉]]、[[鉄刀]]、[[鉄剣]]、銅鈴、[[土師器]]片、[[須恵器]]破片などが出土している。 == 赤烏元年鏡 == [[ファイル:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|対置式神獣鏡

（国の[[重要文化財]]）}}{{small|外区1時半-3時に時計回りに「赤烏元年」銘。[[東京国立博物館]]展示。}}]] 赤烏元年鏡は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定され、[[一宮浅間神社]]（市川三郷町）に所蔵されている（[[東京国立博物館]]に[[寄託 (日本法)|寄託]]）。直径12.5[[センチメートル]]の[[紀年銘鏡]]で、大陸南部の[[呉 (三国)|呉]]の年号である[[赤烏]]元年（[[238年]]）の紀年銘をもつ赤烏の年号を持つものとしては他に安倉高塚古墳（[[宝塚市]]）出土の画文帯[[神獣鏡]]がある（赤烏七年）。[[日本列島]]では[[弥生時代]]には大陸から[[威信材]]として[[青銅]]製品が移入されており、山梨県内でも{{関連仮リンク|貨泉|ref=日本の書道史#貨泉}}や舶載鏡や出土している。大陸で[[遼東半島]]地域を支配していた[[公孫氏]]は[[魏 (三国)|魏]]と敵対する一方で呉と外交関係があったが、鏡に記されている赤烏元年には高句麗と魏の攻撃で[[公孫氏]]は滅ぼされている。『[[魏志倭人伝]]』によれば、日本列島では翌年に[[邪馬台国]]の女王[[卑弥呼]]が魏に[[朝貢]]して[[親魏倭王]]の称号を与えられたといわれ、こうした[[東アジア]]の政治状況のなかで、紀年銘鏡は呉と[[倭国]]間の外交関係、あるいは公孫氏を介しての通交によりもたらされたと考えられている（宮野淳一による）。 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)]] * [[曽根丘陵]] {{デフォルトソート:とりいはらきつねつかこふん}} [[Category:山梨県の古墳]] [[Category:市川三郷町の歴史]] [[Category:山梨県の重要文化財]] [[Category:円墳]]

陶磁片ほか |史跡=なし |特記事項= }} '''鳥居原狐塚古墳'''（とりいばらきつねづかこふん、'''鳥居原古墳'''）は、[[山梨県]][[市川三郷町]]大塚にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。史跡指定はされていない。 == 概要 == [[甲府盆地]]南部、市川三郷町域の北東端に位置。[[曽根丘陵]]西端の支丘上に立地する。[[標高]]は320[[メートル]]付近。曽根丘陵は[[東海地方]]の弥生文化や[[畿内]]の古墳文化が流入した地域で、大塚古墳や[[伊勢塚古墳 (市川三郷町)|伊勢塚古墳]]などが分布する。 [[開墾]]による破壊が激しいが、[[1894年]]（[[明治]]27年）の調査で[[石室]]が確認され、[[昭和]]初年には仁科義男による調査が行われている。内部主体は割石小口積みの[[竪穴式石室]]で、内部には朱が塗布されている。[[埴輪]]は確認されていない。[[1979年]]（昭和54年）に[[発掘調査]]が行われ、[[副葬品]]として仿製[[内行花文鏡]]、舶載の[[神獣鏡]]（対置式[[赤烏]]元年紀年銘鏡）、[[滑石]]製[[臼玉]]、[[鉄刀]]、[[鉄剣]]、銅鈴、[[土師器]]片、[[須恵器]]破片などが出土している。 == 赤烏元年鏡 == [[ファイル:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|対置式神獣鏡
（国の[[重要文化財]]）}}{{small|外区1時半-3時に時計回りに「赤烏元年」銘。[[東京国立博物館]]展示。}}]] 赤烏元年鏡は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定され、[[一宮浅間神社]]（市川三郷町）に所蔵されている（[[東京国立博物館]]に[[寄託 (日本法)|寄託]]）。直径12.5[[センチメートル]]の[[紀年銘鏡]]で、大陸南部の[[呉 (三国)|呉]]の年号である[[赤烏]]元年（[[238年]]）の紀年銘をもつ赤烏の年号を持つものとしては他に[[安倉高塚古墳]]（[[宝塚市]]）出土の画文帯[[神獣鏡]]がある（赤烏七年）。[[日本列島]]では[[弥生時代]]には大陸から[[威信材]]として[[青銅]]製品が移入されており、山梨県内でも{{関連仮リンク|貨泉|ref=日本の書道史#貨泉}}や舶載鏡や出土している。大陸で[[遼東半島]]地域を支配していた[[公孫氏]]は[[魏 (三国)|魏]]と敵対する一方で呉と外交関係があったが、鏡に記されている赤烏元年には高句麗と魏の攻撃で[[公孫氏]]は滅ぼされている。『[[魏志倭人伝]]』によれば、日本列島では翌年に[[邪馬台国]]の女王[[卑弥呼]]が魏に[[朝貢]]して[[親魏倭王]]の称号を与えられたといわれ、こうした[[東アジア]]の政治状況のなかで、紀年銘鏡は呉と[[倭国]]間の外交関係、あるいは公孫氏を介しての通交によりもたらされたと考えられている（宮野淳一による）。 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)]] * [[曽根丘陵]] * [[安倉高塚古墳]] {{デフォルトソート:とりいはらきつねつかこふん}} [[Category:山梨県の古墳]] [[Category:市川三郷町の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:山梨県の重要文化財]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称=鳥居原狐塚古墳 |画像=[[File:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
説明板付近 |別名=鳥居原古墳 |所在地=[[山梨県]][[西八代郡]][[市川三郷町]]大塚 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|34|33.8|N|138|32|45|E|scale:10000}} |形状=[[円墳]] |規模=直径18[[メートル|m]]（推定）
高さ3m |築造時期= |埋葬施設=[[竪穴式石室]] |被葬者= |出土品=[[赤烏]]元年銘[[神獣鏡]]
倣製[[内行花文鏡]]
[[鉄刀]]
陶磁片ほか |史跡=市川三郷町指定史跡「狐塚古墳」 |有形文化財=神獣鏡（国の[[重要文化財]]） |特記事項= }} '''鳥居原狐塚古墳'''（とりいばらきつねづかこふん、'''鳥居原古墳'''）は、[[山梨県]][[市川三郷町]]大塚にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。市川三郷町指定史跡に指定され（指定名称は「狐塚古墳」）、出土神獣鏡は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。 赤鳥元年（[[238年]]）銘神獣鏡の出土で知られる。 == 概要 == [[甲府盆地]]南部、市川三郷町域の北東端に位置。[[曽根丘陵]]西端の支丘上に立地する。[[標高]]は320[[メートル]]付近。曽根丘陵は[[東海地方]]の弥生文化や[[畿内]]の古墳文化が流入した地域で、大塚古墳や[[伊勢塚古墳 (市川三郷町)|伊勢塚古墳]]などが分布する。 [[開墾]]による破壊が激しいが、[[1894年]]（[[明治]]27年）の調査で[[石室]]が確認され、[[昭和]]初年には仁科義男による調査が行われている。内部主体は割石小口積みの[[竪穴式石室]]で、内部には朱が塗布されている。[[埴輪]]は確認されていない。[[1979年]]（昭和54年）に[[発掘調査]]が行われ、[[副葬品]]として仿製[[内行花文鏡]]、舶載の[[神獣鏡]]（対置式[[赤烏]]元年紀年銘鏡）、[[滑石]]製[[臼玉]]、[[鉄刀]]、[[鉄剣]]、銅鈴、[[土師器]]片、[[須恵器]]破片などが出土している。 == 赤烏元年鏡 == [[ファイル:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|対置式神獣鏡
（国の[[重要文化財]]）}}{{small|外区1時半-3時に時計回りに「赤烏元年」銘。[[東京国立博物館]]展示。}}]] 赤烏元年鏡は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定され、[[一宮浅間神社]]（市川三郷町）に所蔵されている（[[東京国立博物館]]に[[寄託 (日本法)|寄託]]）。直径12.5[[センチメートル]]の[[紀年銘鏡]]で、大陸南部の[[呉 (三国)|呉]]の年号である[[赤烏]]元年（[[238年]]）の紀年銘をもつ赤烏の年号を持つものとしては他に[[安倉高塚古墳]]（[[宝塚市]]）出土の画文帯[[神獣鏡]]がある（赤烏七年）。[[日本列島]]では[[弥生時代]]には大陸から[[威信材]]として[[青銅]]製品が移入されており、山梨県内でも{{関連仮リンク|貨泉|ref=日本の書道史#貨泉}}や舶載鏡や出土している。大陸で[[遼東半島]]地域を支配していた[[公孫氏]]は[[魏 (三国)|魏]]と敵対する一方で呉と外交関係があったが、鏡に記されている赤烏元年には高句麗と魏の攻撃で[[公孫氏]]は滅ぼされている。『[[魏志倭人伝]]』によれば、日本列島では翌年に[[邪馬台国]]の女王[[卑弥呼]]が魏に[[朝貢]]して[[親魏倭王]]の称号を与えられたといわれ、こうした[[東アジア]]の政治状況のなかで、紀年銘鏡は呉と[[倭国]]間の外交関係、あるいは公孫氏を介しての通交によりもたらされたと考えられている（宮野淳一による）。 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 神獣鏡 赤鳥元年五月廿五日在銘 山梨県西八代郡三珠町鳥居原狐塚古墳出土（考古資料） - [[一宮浅間神社]]所有、東京国立博物館保管。1979年（昭和54年）6月6日指定。 === 市川三郷町指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 狐塚古墳 - 1976年（昭和51年）6月19日指定。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)]] * [[曽根丘陵]] * [[安倉高塚古墳]] {{デフォルトソート:とりいはらきつねつかこふん}} [[Category:山梨県の古墳]] [[Category:市川三郷町の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:山梨県の重要文化財]] [[Category:山梨県内の市町村指定史跡]]

{{Japanese Burial Mounds | Name = Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun (鳥居原狐塚古墳) | Image = [[File:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Information board near the site | Alternate_name = Toriibara Kofun | Location = {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, {{ill|Nishiyatsushiro District|ja|西八代郡}}, {{ill|Ichikawa Misato|ja|市川三郷町}}, Ōtsuka | Coordinates = {{ill|Geographical coordinates|ja|ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|34|33.8|N|138|32|45|E|scale:10000}} | Shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}} | Size = Diameter: 18 meters (estimated)
Height: 3 meters | Period = | Burial_chamber = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} | Interred = | Artifacts = {{ill|Shenju mirror|ja|神獣鏡}} with an inscription of "Red Bird" 元年 (238 CE)
Replica {{ill|Naiyōka bun kyō|ja|内行花文鏡}}
{{ill|Iron sword|ja|鉄刀}}
Pottery fragments and more | Historic_sites = Designated Municipal Historic Site: "Kitsunezuka Kofun" (Ichikawa Misato Town) | Cultural_properties = Shenju mirror (National Important Cultural Property) | Notes = }} '''Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (鳥居原狐塚古墳), also known as '''Toriibara Kofun''', is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Ōtsuka, {{ill|Ichikawa Misato|ja|市川三郷町}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}. The tumulus is round in shape and has been designated as a Municipal Historic Site under the name "Kitsunezuka Kofun." The Shenju mirror unearthed at the site has been designated as a National Important Cultural Property. The kofun is notable for the discovery of a Shenju mirror bearing an inscription dating to the first year of the Red Bird era (238 CE). == Overview == The kofun is situated in the southern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}}, at the northeastern edge of Ichikawa Misato Town. It stands on the terminal ridge of the Sone Hills at an elevation of approximately 320 meters. The Sone Hills region is known for its cultural interactions between the {{ill|Tōkai Region|ja|東海地方}} during the Yayoi period and the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}} during the Kofun period, with other burial mounds such as the Ōtsuka Kofun and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} distributed nearby. Although the mound has suffered significant destruction due to land clearing, investigations in 1894 (Meiji 27) revealed a stone chamber. Further studies, including one by Yoshio Nishi in the early Shōwa period, confirmed a pit-style stone chamber constructed from neatly piled split stones, with traces of vermilion pigment on the interior. The absence of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was noted. Excavations in 1979 (Shōwa 54) uncovered grave goods including a replica Naiyōka bun kyō, an imported Shenju mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年" (238 CE), talc beads, iron swords, iron daggers, a bronze bell, and fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. == Shenju Mirror with the Red Bird 元年 Inscription == [[File:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shenju mirror with paired depictions (National Important Cultural Property)}}{{small|Inscription: "Red Bird 元年" clockwise in the outer border at 1:30–3:00. Displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, designated as a National Important Cultural Property, is housed at the {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}} in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. It is a 12.5 cm diameter mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年," corresponding to 238 CE, a year in the calendar of the southern Chinese state of {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)}}. In Japan, imported bronze items were regarded as status symbols during the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}, and other imported mirrors have been unearthed in Yamanashi Prefecture. The inscription reflects the geopolitical ties of the era, including Wu's relationship with the {{ill|Gongsun clan|ja|公孫氏}} of the {{ill|Liaodong Peninsula|ja|遼東半島}}, which was destroyed in 238 CE by attacks from {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏}} and {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between {{ill|Yamataikoku|ja|邪馬台国}} and Wei, exemplified by Queen {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}}, housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal Designations === * Historic Site ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)}} * {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}} * {{ill|Ankura Takatsuka Kofun|ja|安倉高塚古墳}} [[Category:Burial mounds in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ichikawa Misato]] [[Category:Round burial mounds]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Japan]] [[ja:鳥居原狐塚古墳]]

Displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, designated as a National Important Cultural Property, is housed at the {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}} in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between {{ill|Yamataikoku|ja|邪馬台国}} and Wei, exemplified by Queen {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}}, housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal Designations === * Historic Site ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)}} * {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}} * {{ill|Ankura Takatsuka Kofun|ja|安倉高塚古墳}} [[Category:Burial mounds in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ichikawa Misato]] [[Category:Round burial mounds]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]][[ja:鳥居原狐塚古墳]]

The tumulus is round in shape and has been designated as a Municipal Historic Site under the name "Kitsunezuka Kofun." The Shenju mirror unearthed at the site has been designated as a National Important Cultural Property. The kofun is notable for the discovery of a Shenju mirror bearing an inscription dating to the first year of the Red Bird era (238 CE). == Overview == The kofun is situated in the southern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}}, at the northeastern edge of Ichikawa Misato Town. It stands on the terminal ridge of the Sone Hills at an elevation of approximately 320 meters. The Sone Hills region is known for its cultural interactions between the {{ill|Tōkai Region|ja|東海地方}} during the Yayoi period and the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}} during the Kofun period, with other burial mounds such as the Ōtsuka Kofun and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} distributed nearby. Although the mound has suffered significant destruction due to land clearing, investigations in 1894 (Meiji 27) revealed a stone chamber. Further studies, including one by Yoshio Nishi in the early Shōwa period, confirmed a pit-style stone chamber constructed from neatly piled split stones, with traces of vermilion pigment on the interior. The absence of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was noted. Excavations in 1979 (Shōwa 54) uncovered grave goods including a replica Naiyōka bun kyō, an imported Shenju mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年" (238 CE), talc beads, iron swords, iron daggers, a bronze bell, and fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. == Shenju Mirror with the Red Bird 元年 Inscription == [[File:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shenju mirror with paired depictions (National Important Cultural Property)}}{{small|Inscription: "Red Bird 元年" clockwise in the outer border at 1:30–3:00. Displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, designated as a National Important Cultural Property, is housed at the {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}} in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. It is a 12.5 cm diameter mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年," corresponding to 238 CE, a year in the calendar of the southern Chinese state of {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)}}. In Japan, imported bronze items were regarded as status symbols during the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}, and other imported mirrors have been unearthed in Yamanashi Prefecture. The inscription reflects the geopolitical ties of the era, including Wu's relationship with the {{ill|Gongsun clan|ja|公孫氏}} of the {{ill|Liaodong Peninsula|ja|遼東半島}}, which was destroyed in 238 CE by attacks from {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏}} and {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between {{ill|Yamataikoku|ja|邪馬台国}} and Wei, exemplified by Queen {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}}, housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal Designations === * Historic Site ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)}} * {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}} * {{ill|Ankura Takatsuka Kofun|ja|安倉高塚古墳}} [[Category:Burial mounds in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ichikawa Misato]] [[Category:Round burial mounds]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]][[ja:鳥居原狐塚古墳]]

According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between {{ill|Yamataikoku|ja|邪馬台国}} and Wei, exemplified by Queen {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. Pottery fragments and more | Historic_sites = Designated Municipal Historic Site: "Kitsunezuka Kofun" (Ichikawa Misato Town) | Cultural_properties = Shenju mirror (National Important Cultural Property) | Notes = }} '''Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (鳥居原狐塚古墳), also known as '''Toriibara Kofun''', is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Ōtsuka, {{ill|Ichikawa Misato|ja|市川三郷町}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}. The tumulus is round in shape and has been designated as a Municipal Historic Site under the name "Kitsunezuka Kofun." The Shenju mirror unearthed at the site has been designated as a National Important Cultural Property. The kofun is notable for the discovery of a Shenju mirror bearing an inscription dating to the first year of the Red Bird era (238 CE). == Overview == The kofun is situated in the southern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}}, at the northeastern edge of Ichikawa Misato Town. It stands on the terminal ridge of the Sone Hills at an elevation of approximately 320 meters. The Sone Hills region is known for its cultural interactions between the {{ill|Tōkai Region|ja|東海地方}} during the Yayoi period and the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}} during the Kofun period, with other burial mounds such as the Ōtsuka Kofun and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} distributed nearby. Although the mound has suffered significant destruction due to land clearing, investigations in 1894 (Meiji 27) revealed a stone chamber. Further studies, including one by Yoshio Nishi in the early Shōwa period, confirmed a pit-style stone chamber constructed from neatly piled split stones, with traces of vermilion pigment on the interior. The absence of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was noted. Excavations in 1979 (Shōwa 54) uncovered grave goods including a replica Naiyōka bun kyō, an imported Shenju mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年" (238 CE), talc beads, iron swords, iron daggers, a bronze bell, and fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. == Shenju Mirror with the Red Bird 元年 Inscription == [[File:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shenju mirror with paired depictions (National Important Cultural Property)}}{{small|Inscription: "Red Bird 元年" clockwise in the outer border at 1:30–3:00. Displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, designated as a National Important Cultural Property, is housed at the {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}} in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. It is a 12.5 cm diameter mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年," corresponding to 238 CE, a year in the calendar of the southern Chinese state of {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)}}. In Japan, imported bronze items were regarded as status symbols during the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}, and other imported mirrors have been unearthed in Yamanashi Prefecture. The inscription reflects the geopolitical ties of the era, including Wu's relationship with the {{ill|Gongsun clan|ja|公孫氏}} of the {{ill|Liaodong Peninsula|ja|遼東半島}}, which was destroyed in 238 CE by attacks from {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏}} and {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between {{ill|Yamataikoku|ja|邪馬台国}} and Wei, exemplified by Queen {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}}, housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal Designations === * Historic Site ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)}} * {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}} * {{ill|Ankura Takatsuka Kofun|ja|安倉高塚古墳}} [[Category:Burial mounds in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ichikawa Misato]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Images]] [[ja:鳥居原狐塚古墳]]

The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}}, housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. {{Japanese Burial Mounds | Name = Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun (鳥居原狐塚古墳) | Image = [[File:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Information board near the site | Alternate_name = Toriibara Kofun | Location = {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, {{ill|Nishiyatsushiro District|ja|西八代郡}}, {{ill|Ichikawa Misato|ja|市川三郷町}}, Ōtsuka | Coordinates = {{ill|Geographical coordinates|ja|ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|34|33.8|N|138|32|45|E|scale:10000}} | Shape = {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}} | Size = Diameter: 18 meters (estimated)
Height: 3 meters | Period = | Burial_chamber = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} | Interred = | Artifacts = {{ill|Shenju mirror|ja|神獣鏡}} with an inscription of "Red Bird" 元年 (238 CE)
Replica {{ill|Naiyōka bun kyō|ja|内行花文鏡}}
{{ill|Iron sword|ja|鉄刀}}
Pottery fragments and more | Historic_sites = Designated Municipal Historic Site: "Kitsunezuka Kofun" (Ichikawa Misato Town) | Cultural_properties = Shenju mirror (National Important Cultural Property) | Notes = }} '''Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (鳥居原狐塚古墳), also known as '''Toriibara Kofun''', is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Ōtsuka, {{ill|Ichikawa Misato|ja|市川三郷町}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}. The tumulus is round in shape and has been designated as a Municipal Historic Site under the name "Kitsunezuka Kofun." The Shenju mirror unearthed at the site has been designated as a National Important Cultural Property. The kofun is notable for the discovery of a Shenju mirror bearing an inscription dating to the first year of the Red Bird era (238 CE). == Overview == The kofun is situated in the southern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}}, at the northeastern edge of Ichikawa Misato Town. It stands on the terminal ridge of the Sone Hills at an elevation of approximately 320 meters. The Sone Hills region is known for its cultural interactions between the {{ill|Tōkai Region|ja|東海地方}} during the Yayoi period and the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}} during the Kofun period, with other burial mounds such as the Ōtsuka Kofun and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} distributed nearby. Although the mound has suffered significant destruction due to land clearing, investigations in 1894 (Meiji 27) revealed a stone chamber. Further studies, including one by Yoshio Nishi in the early Shōwa period, confirmed a pit-style stone chamber constructed from neatly piled split stones, with traces of vermilion pigment on the interior. The absence of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was noted. Excavations in 1979 (Shōwa 54) uncovered grave goods including a replica Naiyōka bun kyō, an imported Shenju mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年" (238 CE), talc beads, iron swords, iron daggers, a bronze bell, and fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. == Shenju Mirror with the Red Bird 元年 Inscription == [[File:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shenju mirror with paired depictions (National Important Cultural Property)}}{{small|Inscription: "Red Bird 元年" clockwise in the outer border at 1:30–3:00. Displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, designated as a National Important Cultural Property, is housed at the {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}} in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. It is a 12.5 cm diameter mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年," corresponding to 238 CE, a year in the calendar of the southern Chinese state of {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)}}. In Japan, imported bronze items were regarded as status symbols during the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}, and other imported mirrors have been unearthed in Yamanashi Prefecture. The inscription reflects the geopolitical ties of the era, including Wu's relationship with the {{ill|Gongsun clan|ja|公孫氏}} of the {{ill|Liaodong Peninsula|ja|遼東半島}}, which was destroyed in 238 CE by attacks from {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏}} and {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between {{ill|Yamataikoku|ja|邪馬台国}} and Wei, exemplified by Queen {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}}, housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal Designations === * Historic Site ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)}} * {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}} * {{ill|Ankura Takatsuka Kofun|ja|安倉高塚古墳}} [[Category:Burial mounds in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ichikawa Misato]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Images]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]] [[ja:鳥居原狐塚古墳]]

{{Japanese Burial Mounds | Name = Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun (鳥居原狐塚古墳) | Image = [[File:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]

Height: 3 meters | Period = | Burial_chamber = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} | Interred = | Artifacts = {{ill|Shenju mirror|ja|神獣鏡}} with an inscription of "Red Bird" 元年 (238 CE)
Replica {{ill|Naiyōka bun kyō|ja|内行花文鏡}}
{{ill|Iron sword|ja|鉄刀}}
Pottery fragments and more | Historic_sites = Designated Municipal Historic Site: "Kitsunezuka Kofun" (Ichikawa Misato Town) | Cultural_properties = Shenju mirror (National Important Cultural Property) | Notes = }} '''Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (鳥居原狐塚古墳), also known as '''Toriibara Kofun''', is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Ōtsuka, {{ill|Ichikawa Misato|ja|市川三郷町}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}. The tumulus is round in shape and has been designated as a Municipal Historic Site under the name "Kitsunezuka Kofun." The Shenju mirror unearthed at the site has been designated as a National Important Cultural Property. The kofun is notable for the discovery of a Shenju mirror bearing an inscription dating to the first year of the Red Bird era (238 CE). == Overview == The kofun is situated in the southern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}}, at the northeastern edge of Ichikawa Misato Town. It stands on the terminal ridge of the Sone Hills at an elevation of approximately 320 meters. The Sone Hills region is known for its cultural interactions between the {{ill|Tōkai Region|ja|東海地方}} during the Yayoi period and the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}} during the Kofun period, with other burial mounds such as the Ōtsuka Kofun and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} distributed nearby. Although the mound has suffered significant destruction due to land clearing, investigations in 1894 (Meiji 27) revealed a stone chamber. Further studies, including one by Yoshio Nishi in the early Shōwa period, confirmed a pit-style stone chamber constructed from neatly piled split stones, with traces of vermilion pigment on the interior. The absence of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was noted. Excavations in 1979 (Shōwa 54) uncovered grave goods including a replica Naiyōka bun kyō, an imported Shenju mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年" (238 CE), talc beads, iron swords, iron daggers, a bronze bell, and fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. == Shenju Mirror with the Red Bird 元年 Inscription == [[File:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shenju mirror with paired depictions (National Important Cultural Property)}}{{small|Inscription: "Red Bird 元年" clockwise in the outer border at 1:30–3:00. Displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, designated as a National Important Cultural Property, is housed at the {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}} in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. It is a 12.5 cm diameter mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年," corresponding to 238 CE, a year in the calendar of the southern Chinese state of {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)}}. In Japan, imported bronze items were regarded as status symbols during the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}, and other imported mirrors have been unearthed in Yamanashi Prefecture. The inscription reflects the geopolitical ties of the era, including Wu's relationship with the {{ill|Gongsun clan|ja|公孫氏}} of the {{ill|Liaodong Peninsula|ja|遼東半島}}, which was destroyed in 238 CE by attacks from {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏}} and {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between {{ill|Yamataikoku|ja|邪馬台国}} and Wei, exemplified by Queen {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}}, housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal Designations === * Historic Site ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)}} * {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}} * {{ill|Ankura Takatsuka Kofun|ja|安倉高塚古墳}} [[Category:Burial mounds in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ichikawa Misato]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[Category:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[ja:鳥居原狐塚古墳]]

revision.comment: /* 赤烏元年鏡 */ [[貨泉]]記事作成に伴う作業
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=鳥居原狐塚古墳 |画像=[[File:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
説明板付近 |別名=鳥居原古墳 |所在地=[[山梨県]][[西八代郡]][[市川三郷町]]大塚 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|34|33.8|N|138|32|45|E|scale:10000}} |形状=[[円墳]] |規模=直径18[[メートル|m]]（推定）
高さ3m |築造時期= |埋葬施設=[[竪穴式石室]] |被葬者= |出土品=[[赤烏]]元年銘[[神獣鏡]]
倣製[[内行花文鏡]]
[[鉄刀]]
陶磁片ほか |史跡=市川三郷町指定史跡「狐塚古墳」 |有形文化財=神獣鏡（国の[[重要文化財]]） |特記事項= }} '''鳥居原狐塚古墳'''（とりいばらきつねづかこふん、'''鳥居原古墳'''）は、[[山梨県]][[市川三郷町]]大塚にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。市川三郷町指定史跡に指定され（指定名称は「狐塚古墳」）、出土神獣鏡は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。 赤鳥元年（[[238年]]）銘神獣鏡の出土で知られる。 == 概要 == [[甲府盆地]]南部、市川三郷町域の北東端に位置。[[曽根丘陵]]西端の支丘上に立地する。[[標高]]は320[[メートル]]付近。曽根丘陵は[[東海地方]]の弥生文化や[[畿内]]の古墳文化が流入した地域で、大塚古墳や[[伊勢塚古墳 (市川三郷町)|伊勢塚古墳]]などが分布する。 [[開墾]]による破壊が激しいが、[[1894年]]（[[明治]]27年）の調査で[[石室]]が確認され、[[昭和]]初年には仁科義男による調査が行われている。内部主体は割石小口積みの[[竪穴式石室]]で、内部には朱が塗布されている。[[埴輪]]は確認されていない。[[1979年]]（昭和54年）に[[発掘調査]]が行われ、[[副葬品]]として仿製[[内行花文鏡]]、舶載の[[神獣鏡]]（対置式[[赤烏]]元年紀年銘鏡）、[[滑石]]製[[臼玉]]、[[鉄刀]]、[[鉄剣]]、銅鈴、[[土師器]]片、[[須恵器]]破片などが出土している。 == 赤烏元年鏡 == [[ファイル:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|対置式神獣鏡
（国の[[重要文化財]]）}}{{small|外区1時半-3時に時計回りに「赤烏元年」銘。[[東京国立博物館]]展示。}}]] 赤烏元年鏡は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定され、[[一宮浅間神社]]（市川三郷町）に所蔵されている（[[東京国立博物館]]に[[寄託 (日本法)|寄託]]）。直径12.5[[センチメートル]]の[[紀年銘鏡]]で、大陸南部の[[呉 (三国)|呉]]の年号である[[赤烏]]元年（[[238年]]）の紀年銘をもつ赤烏の年号を持つものとしては他に[[安倉高塚古墳]]（[[宝塚市]]）出土の画文帯[[神獣鏡]]がある（赤烏七年）。[[日本列島]]では[[弥生時代]]には大陸から[[威信材]]として[[青銅]]製品が移入されており、山梨県内でも[[貨泉]]や舶載鏡や出土している。大陸で[[遼東半島]]地域を支配していた[[公孫氏]]は[[魏 (三国)|魏]]と敵対する一方で呉と外交関係があったが、鏡に記されている赤烏元年には高句麗と魏の攻撃で[[公孫氏]]は滅ぼされている。『[[魏志倭人伝]]』によれば、日本列島では翌年に[[邪馬台国]]の女王[[卑弥呼]]が魏に[[朝貢]]して[[親魏倭王]]の称号を与えられたといわれ、こうした[[東アジア]]の政治状況のなかで、紀年銘鏡は呉と[[倭国]]間の外交関係、あるいは公孫氏を介しての通交によりもたらされたと考えられている（宮野淳一による）。 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 神獣鏡 赤鳥元年五月廿五日在銘 山梨県西八代郡三珠町鳥居原狐塚古墳出土（考古資料） - [[一宮浅間神社]]所有、東京国立博物館保管。1979年（昭和54年）6月6日指定。 === 市川三郷町指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 狐塚古墳 - 1976年（昭和51年）6月19日指定。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)]] * [[曽根丘陵]] * [[安倉高塚古墳]] {{デフォルトソート:とりいはらきつねつかこふん}} [[Category:山梨県の古墳]] [[Category:市川三郷町の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:山梨県の重要文化財]] [[Category:山梨県内の市町村指定史跡]]

Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal Designations === * Historic Site ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)}} * {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}} * {{ill|Ankura Takatsuka Kofun|ja|安倉高塚古墳}} [[Category:Burial mounds in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ichikawa Misato]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[Category:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[ja:鳥居原狐塚古墳]]

Pottery fragments and more | Historic_sites = Designated Municipal Historic Site: "Kitsunezuka Kofun" (Ichikawa Misato Town) | Cultural_properties = Shenju mirror (National Important Cultural Property) | Notes = }} '''Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (鳥居原狐塚古墳), also known as '''Toriibara Kofun''', is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Ōtsuka, {{ill|Ichikawa Misato|ja|市川三郷町}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}. The tumulus is round in shape and has been designated as a Municipal Historic Site under the name "Kitsunezuka Kofun." The Shenju mirror unearthed at the site has been designated as a National Important Cultural Property. The kofun is notable for the discovery of a Shenju mirror bearing an inscription dating to the first year of the Red Bird era (238 CE). == Overview == The kofun is situated in the southern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}}, at the northeastern edge of Ichikawa Misato Town. It stands on the terminal ridge of the Sone Hills at an elevation of approximately 320 meters. The Sone Hills region is known for its cultural interactions between the {{ill|Tōkai Region|ja|東海地方}} during the Yayoi period and the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}} during the Kofun period, with other burial mounds such as the Ōtsuka Kofun and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} distributed nearby. Although the mound has suffered significant destruction due to land clearing, investigations in 1894 (Meiji 27) revealed a stone chamber. Further studies, including one by Yoshio Nishi in the early Shōwa period, confirmed a pit-style stone chamber constructed from neatly piled split stones, with traces of vermilion pigment on the interior. The absence of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was noted. Excavations in 1979 (Shōwa 54) uncovered grave goods including a replica Naiyōka bun kyō, an imported Shenju mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年" (238 CE), talc beads, iron swords, iron daggers, a bronze bell, and fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. == Shenju Mirror with the Red Bird 元年 Inscription == [[File:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shenju mirror with paired depictions (National Important Cultural Property)}}{{small|Inscription: "Red Bird 元年" clockwise in the outer border at 1:30–3:00. Displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, designated as a National Important Cultural Property, is housed at the {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}} in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. It is a 12.5 cm diameter mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年," corresponding to 238 CE, a year in the calendar of the southern Chinese state of {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)}}. In Japan, imported bronze items were regarded as status symbols during the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}, and other imported mirrors have been unearthed in Yamanashi Prefecture. The inscription reflects the geopolitical ties of the era, including Wu's relationship with the {{ill|Gongsun clan|ja|公孫氏}} of the {{ill|Liaodong Peninsula|ja|遼東半島}}, which was destroyed in 238 CE by attacks from {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏}} and {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between {{ill|Yamataikoku|ja|邪馬台国}} and Wei, exemplified by Queen {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}}, housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal Designations === * Historic Site ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)}} * {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}} * {{ill|Ankura Takatsuka Kofun|ja|安倉高塚古墳}} [[Category:Burial mounds in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ichikawa Misato]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[ja:鳥居原狐塚古墳]]

Pottery fragments and more | Historic_sites = Designated Municipal Historic Site: "Kitsunezuka Kofun" (Ichikawa Misato Town) | Cultural_properties = Shenju mirror (National Important Cultural Property) | Notes = }} '''Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (鳥居原狐塚古墳), also known as '''Toriibara Kofun''', is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Ōtsuka, {{ill|Ichikawa Misato|ja|市川三郷町}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}. The tumulus is round in shape and has been designated as a Municipal Historic Site under the name "Kitsunezuka Kofun." The Shenju mirror unearthed at the site has been designated as a National Important Cultural Property. The kofun is notable for the discovery of a Shenju mirror bearing an inscription dating to the first year of the Red Bird era (238 CE). == Overview == The kofun is situated in the southern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}}, at the northeastern edge of Ichikawa Misato Town. It stands on the terminal ridge of the Sone Hills at an elevation of approximately 320 meters. The Sone Hills region is known for its cultural interactions between the {{ill|Tōkai Region|ja|東海地方}} during the Yayoi period and the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}} during the Kofun period, with other burial mounds such as the Ōtsuka Kofun and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} distributed nearby. Although the mound has suffered significant destruction due to land clearing, investigations in 1894 (Meiji 27) revealed a stone chamber. Further studies, including one by Yoshio Nishi in the early Shōwa period, confirmed a pit-style stone chamber constructed from neatly piled split stones, with traces of vermilion pigment on the interior. The absence of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was noted. Excavations in 1979 (Shōwa 54) uncovered grave goods including a replica Naiyōka bun kyō, an imported Shenju mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年" (238 CE), talc beads, iron swords, iron daggers, a bronze bell, and fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. == Shenju Mirror with the Red Bird 元年 Inscription == [[File:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shenju mirror with paired depictions (National Important Cultural Property)}}{{small|Inscription: "Red Bird 元年" clockwise in the outer border at 1:30–3:00. Displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, designated as a National Important Cultural Property, is housed at the {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}} in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. It is a 12.5 cm diameter mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年," corresponding to 238 CE, a year in the calendar of the southern Chinese state of {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)}}. In Japan, imported bronze items were regarded as status symbols during the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}, and other imported mirrors have been unearthed in Yamanashi Prefecture. The inscription reflects the geopolitical ties of the era, including Wu's relationship with the {{ill|Gongsun clan|ja|公孫氏}} of the {{ill|Liaodong Peninsula|ja|遼東半島}}, which was destroyed in 238 CE by attacks from {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏}} and {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between {{ill|Yamataikoku|ja|邪馬台国}} and Wei, exemplified by Queen {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}}, housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. Pottery fragments and more | Historic_sites = Designated Municipal Historic Site: "Kitsunezuka Kofun" (Ichikawa Misato Town) | Cultural_properties = Shenju mirror (National Important Cultural Property) | Notes = }} '''Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (鳥居原狐塚古墳), also known as '''Toriibara Kofun''', is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Ōtsuka, {{ill|Ichikawa Misato|ja|市川三郷町}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}. The tumulus is round in shape and has been designated as a Municipal Historic Site under the name "Kitsunezuka Kofun." The Shenju mirror unearthed at the site has been designated as a National Important Cultural Property. The kofun is notable for the discovery of a Shenju mirror bearing an inscription dating to the first year of the Red Bird era (238 CE). == Overview == The kofun is situated in the southern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}}, at the northeastern edge of Ichikawa Misato Town. It stands on the terminal ridge of the Sone Hills at an elevation of approximately 320 meters. The Sone Hills region is known for its cultural interactions between the {{ill|Tōkai Region|ja|東海地方}} during the Yayoi period and the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}} during the Kofun period, with other burial mounds such as the Ōtsuka Kofun and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} distributed nearby. Although the mound has suffered significant destruction due to land clearing, investigations in 1894 (Meiji 27) revealed a stone chamber. Further studies, including one by Yoshio Nishi in the early Shōwa period, confirmed a pit-style stone chamber constructed from neatly piled split stones, with traces of vermilion pigment on the interior. The absence of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was noted. Excavations in 1979 (Shōwa 54) uncovered grave goods including a replica Naiyōka bun kyō, an imported Shenju mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年" (238 CE), talc beads, iron swords, iron daggers, a bronze bell, and fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. == Shenju Mirror with the Red Bird 元年 Inscription == [[File:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shenju mirror with paired depictions (National Important Cultural Property)}}{{small|Inscription: "Red Bird 元年" clockwise in the outer border at 1:30–3:00. Displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, designated as a National Important Cultural Property, is housed at the {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}} in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. It is a 12.5 cm diameter mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年," corresponding to 238 CE, a year in the calendar of the southern Chinese state of {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)}}. In Japan, imported bronze items were regarded as status symbols during the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}, and other imported mirrors have been unearthed in Yamanashi Prefecture. The inscription reflects the geopolitical ties of the era, including Wu's relationship with the {{ill|Gongsun clan|ja|公孫氏}} of the {{ill|Liaodong Peninsula|ja|遼東半島}}, which was destroyed in 238 CE by attacks from {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏}} and {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between {{ill|Yamataikoku|ja|邪馬台国}} and Wei, exemplified by Queen {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}}, housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. Pottery fragments and more | Historic_sites = Designated Municipal Historic Site: "Kitsunezuka Kofun" (Ichikawa Misato Town) | Cultural_properties = Shenju mirror (National Important Cultural Property) | Notes = }} '''Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (鳥居原狐塚古墳), also known as '''Toriibara Kofun''', is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Ōtsuka, {{ill|Ichikawa Misato|ja|市川三郷町}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}. The tumulus is round in shape and has been designated as a Municipal Historic Site under the name "Kitsunezuka Kofun." The Shenju mirror unearthed at the site has been designated as a National Important Cultural Property. The kofun is notable for the discovery of a Shenju mirror bearing an inscription dating to the first year of the Red Bird era (238 CE). == Overview == The kofun is situated in the southern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}}, at the northeastern edge of Ichikawa Misato Town. It stands on the terminal ridge of the Sone Hills at an elevation of approximately 320 meters. The Sone Hills region is known for its cultural interactions between the {{ill|Tōkai Region|ja|東海地方}} during the Yayoi period and the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}} during the Kofun period, with other burial mounds such as the Ōtsuka Kofun and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} distributed nearby. Although the mound has suffered significant destruction due to land clearing, investigations in 1894 (Meiji 27) revealed a stone chamber. Further studies, including one by Yoshio Nishi in the early Shōwa period, confirmed a pit-style stone chamber constructed from neatly piled split stones, with traces of vermilion pigment on the interior. The absence of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was noted. Excavations in 1979 (Shōwa 54) uncovered grave goods including a replica Naiyōka bun kyō, an imported Shenju mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年" (238 CE), talc beads, iron swords, iron daggers, a bronze bell, and fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. == Shenju Mirror with the Red Bird 元年 Inscription == [[File:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shenju mirror with paired depictions (National Important Cultural Property)}}{{small|Inscription: "Red Bird 元年" clockwise in the outer border at 1:30–3:00. Displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, designated as a National Important Cultural Property, is housed at the {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}} in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. It is a 12.5 cm diameter mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年," corresponding to 238 CE, a year in the calendar of the southern Chinese state of {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)}}. In Japan, imported bronze items were regarded as status symbols during the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}, and other imported mirrors have been unearthed in Yamanashi Prefecture. The inscription reflects the geopolitical ties of the era, including Wu's relationship with the {{ill|Gongsun clan|ja|公孫氏}} of the {{ill|Liaodong Peninsula|ja|遼東半島}}, which was destroyed in 238 CE by attacks from {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏}} and {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between {{ill|Yamataikoku|ja|邪馬台国}} and Wei, exemplified by Queen {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}}, housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal Designations === * Historic Site ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)}} * {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}} * {{ill|Ankura Takatsuka Kofun|ja|安倉高塚古墳}} [[Category:Burial mounds in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ichikawa Misato]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun-qq]] [[Category:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[ja:鳥居原狐塚古墳]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

Pottery fragments and more | Historic_sites = Designated Municipal Historic Site: "Kitsunezuka Kofun" (Ichikawa Misato Town) | Cultural_properties = Shenju mirror (National Important Cultural Property) | Notes = }} '''Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (鳥居原狐塚古墳), also known as '''Toriibara Kofun''', is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Ōtsuka, {{ill|Ichikawa Misato|ja|市川三郷町}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}. The tumulus is round in shape and has been designated as a Municipal Historic Site under the name "Kitsunezuka Kofun." The Shenju mirror unearthed at the site has been designated as a National Important Cultural Property. The kofun is notable for the discovery of a Shenju mirror bearing an inscription dating to the first year of the Red Bird era (238 CE). == Overview == The kofun is situated in the southern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}}, at the northeastern edge of Ichikawa Misato Town. It stands on the terminal ridge of the Sone Hills at an elevation of approximately 320 meters. The Sone Hills region is known for its cultural interactions between the {{ill|Tōkai Region|ja|東海地方}} during the Yayoi period and the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}} during the Kofun period, with other burial mounds such as the Ōtsuka Kofun and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} distributed nearby. Although the mound has suffered significant destruction due to land clearing, investigations in 1894 (Meiji 27) revealed a stone chamber. Further studies, including one by Yoshio Nishi in the early Shōwa period, confirmed a pit-style stone chamber constructed from neatly piled split stones, with traces of vermilion pigment on the interior. The absence of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was noted. Excavations in 1979 (Shōwa 54) uncovered grave goods including a replica Naiyōka bun kyō, an imported Shenju mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年" (238 CE), talc beads, iron swords, iron daggers, a bronze bell, and fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. == Shenju Mirror with the Red Bird 元年 Inscription == [[File:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shenju mirror with paired depictions (National Important Cultural Property)}}{{small|Inscription: "Red Bird 元年" clockwise in the outer border at 1:30–3:00. Displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, designated as a National Important Cultural Property, is housed at the {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}} in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. It is a 12.5 cm diameter mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年," corresponding to 238 CE, a year in the calendar of the southern Chinese state of {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)}}. In Japan, imported bronze items were regarded as status symbols during the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}, and other imported mirrors have been unearthed in Yamanashi Prefecture. The inscription reflects the geopolitical ties of the era, including Wu's relationship with the {{ill|Gongsun clan|ja|公孫氏}} of the {{ill|Liaodong Peninsula|ja|遼東半島}}, which was destroyed in 238 CE by attacks from {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏}} and {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between {{ill|Yamataikoku|ja|邪馬台国}} and Wei, exemplified by Queen {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}}, housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal Designations === * Historic Site ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)}} * {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}} * {{ill|Ankura Takatsuka Kofun|ja|安倉高塚古墳}} [[Category:Burial mounds in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ichikawa The kofun is notable for the discovery of a Shenju mirror bearing an inscription dating to the first year of the Red Bird era (238 CE). == Overview == The kofun is situated in the southern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}}, at the northeastern edge of Ichikawa Misato Town. It stands on the terminal ridge of the Sone Hills at an elevation of approximately 320 meters. The Sone Hills region is known for its cultural interactions between the {{ill|Tōkai Region|ja|東海地方}} during the Yayoi period and the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}} during the Kofun period, with other burial mounds such as the Ōtsuka Kofun and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} distributed nearby. Although the mound has suffered significant destruction due to land clearing, investigations in 1894 (Meiji 27) revealed a stone chamber. Further studies, including one by Yoshio Nishi in the early Shōwa period, confirmed a pit-style stone chamber constructed from neatly piled split stones, with traces of vermilion pigment on the interior. The absence of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was noted. Excavations in 1979 (Shōwa 54) uncovered grave goods including a replica Naiyōka bun kyō, an imported Shenju mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年" (238 CE), talc beads, iron swords, iron daggers, a bronze bell, and fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. == Shenju Mirror with the Red Bird 元年 Inscription == [[File:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shenju mirror with paired depictions (National Important Cultural Property)}}{{small|Inscription: "Red Bird 元年" clockwise in the outer border at 1:30–3:00. Displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, designated as a National Important Cultural Property, is housed at the {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}} in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. It is a 12.5 cm diameter mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年," corresponding to 238 CE, a year in the calendar of the southern Chinese state of {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)}}. In Japan, imported bronze items were regarded as status symbols during the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}, and other imported mirrors have been unearthed in Yamanashi Prefecture. The inscription reflects the geopolitical ties of the era, including Wu's relationship with the {{ill|Gongsun clan|ja|公孫氏}} of the {{ill|Liaodong Peninsula|ja|遼東半島}}, which was destroyed in 238 CE by attacks from {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏}} and {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between {{ill|Yamataikoku|ja|邪馬台国}} and Wei, exemplified by Queen {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}}, housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal Designations === * Historic Site ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)}} * {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}} * {{ill|Ankura Takatsuka Kofun|ja|安倉高塚古墳}} [[Category:Burial mounds in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ichikawa Excavations in 1979 (Shōwa 54) uncovered grave goods including a replica Naiyōka bun kyō, an imported Shenju mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年" (238 CE), talc beads, iron swords, iron daggers, a bronze bell, and fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. == Shenju Mirror with the Red Bird 元年 Inscription == [[File:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shenju mirror with paired depictions (National Important Cultural Property)}}{{small|Inscription: "Red Bird 元年" clockwise in the outer border at 1:30–3:00. Displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, designated as a National Important Cultural Property, is housed at the {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}} in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. It is a 12.5 cm diameter mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年," corresponding to 238 CE, a year in the calendar of the southern Chinese state of {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)}}. In Japan, imported bronze items were regarded as status symbols during the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}, and other imported mirrors have been unearthed in Yamanashi Prefecture. The inscription reflects the geopolitical ties of the era, including Wu's relationship with the {{ill|Gongsun clan|ja|公孫氏}} of the {{ill|Liaodong Peninsula|ja|遼東半島}}, which was destroyed in 238 CE by attacks from {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏}} and {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between {{ill|Yamataikoku|ja|邪馬台国}} and Wei, exemplified by Queen {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}}, housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. Pottery fragments and more | Historic_sites = Designated Municipal Historic Site: "Kitsunezuka Kofun" (Ichikawa Misato Town) | Cultural_properties = Shenju mirror (National Important Cultural Property) | Notes = }} '''Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (鳥居原狐塚古墳), also known as '''Toriibara Kofun''', is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Ōtsuka, {{ill|Ichikawa Misato|ja|市川三郷町}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}. The tumulus is round in shape and has been designated as a Municipal Historic Site under the name "Kitsunezuka Kofun." The Shenju mirror unearthed at the site has been designated as a National Important Cultural Property. The kofun is notable for the discovery of a Shenju mirror bearing an inscription dating to the first year of the Red Bird era (238 CE). == Overview == The kofun is situated in the southern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}}, at the northeastern edge of Ichikawa Misato Town. It stands on the terminal ridge of the Sone Hills at an elevation of approximately 320 meters. The Sone Hills region is known for its cultural interactions between the {{ill|Tōkai Region|ja|東海地方}} during the Yayoi period and the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}} during the Kofun period, with other burial mounds such as the Ōtsuka Kofun and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} distributed nearby. Although the mound has suffered significant destruction due to land clearing, investigations in 1894 (Meiji 27) revealed a stone chamber. Further studies, including one by Yoshio Nishi in the early Shōwa period, confirmed a pit-style stone chamber constructed from neatly piled split stones, with traces of vermilion pigment on the interior. The absence of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was noted. Excavations in 1979 (Shōwa 54) uncovered grave goods including a replica Naiyōka bun kyō, an imported Shenju mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年" (238 CE), talc beads, iron swords, iron daggers, a bronze bell, and fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. == Shenju Mirror with the Red Bird 元年 Inscription == [[File:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shenju mirror with paired depictions (National Important Cultural Property)}}{{small|Inscription: "Red Bird 元年" clockwise in the outer border at 1:30–3:00. Displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, designated as a National Important Cultural Property, is housed at the {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}} in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. It is a 12.5 cm diameter mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年," corresponding to 238 CE, a year in the calendar of the southern Chinese state of {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)}}. In Japan, imported bronze items were regarded as status symbols during the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}, and other imported mirrors have been unearthed in Yamanashi Prefecture. The inscription reflects the geopolitical ties of the era, including Wu's relationship with the {{ill|Gongsun clan|ja|公孫氏}} of the {{ill|Liaodong Peninsula|ja|遼東半島}}, which was destroyed in 238 CE by attacks from {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏}} and {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between {{ill|Yamataikoku|ja|邪馬台国}} and Wei, exemplified by Queen {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}}, housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal Designations === * Historic Site ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Excavations in 1979 (Shōwa 54) uncovered grave goods including a replica Naiyōka bun kyō, an imported Shenju mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年" (238 CE), talc beads, iron swords, iron daggers, a bronze bell, and fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}} and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. == Shenju Mirror with the Red Bird 元年 Inscription == [[File:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shenju mirror with paired depictions (National Important Cultural Property)}}{{small|Inscription: "Red Bird 元年" clockwise in the outer border at 1:30–3:00. Displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, designated as a National Important Cultural Property, is housed at the {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}} in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. It is a 12.5 cm diameter mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年," corresponding to 238 CE, a year in the calendar of the southern Chinese state of {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)}}. In Japan, imported bronze items were regarded as status symbols during the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}, and other imported mirrors have been unearthed in Yamanashi Prefecture. The inscription reflects the geopolitical ties of the era, including Wu's relationship with the {{ill|Gongsun clan|ja|公孫氏}} of the {{ill|Liaodong Peninsula|ja|遼東半島}}, which was destroyed in 238 CE by attacks from {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏}} and {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between {{ill|Yamataikoku|ja|邪馬台国}} and Wei, exemplified by Queen {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}}, housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal Designations === * Historic Site ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)}} * {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}} * {{ill|Ankura Takatsuka Kofun|ja|安倉高塚古墳}} [[Category:Burial mounds in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ichikawa
