Pottery fragments and more | Historic_sites = Designated Municipal other_designation: "Kitsunezuka Kofun" (Ichikawa Misato Town) | Cultural_properties = Shenju mirror (National Important Cultural Property) | notes = }} '''Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (鳥居原狐塚古墳), also known as '''Toriibara Kofun''', is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Ōtsuka, {{ill|Ichikawa Misato|ja|市川三郷町}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}. The tumulus is round in type and has been other_designation1 as a Municipal other_designation under the name "Kitsunezuka Kofun." The Shenju mirror unearthed at the site has been other_designation1 as a National Important Cultural Property. The kofun is notable for the discovery of a Shenju mirror bearing an inscription dating to the first year of the Red Bird era (238 CE). == Overview == The kofun is situated in the southern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}}, at the northeastern edge of Ichikawa Misato Town. It stands on the terminal ridge of the Sone Hills at an elevation of approximately 320 meters. The Sone Hills region is known for its cultural interactions between the {{ill|Tōkai Region|ja|東海地方}} during the Yayoi period and the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}} during the Kofun period, with other Kofuns such as the Ōtsuka Kofun and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} distributed nearby. Although the mound has suffered significant destruction due to land clearing, investigations in 1894 (Meiji 27) revealed a stone chamber. Further studies, including one by Yoshio Nishi in the early Shōwa period, confirmed a pit-style stone chamber constructed from neatly piled split stones, with traces of vermilion pigment on the interior. The absence of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was noted. Excavations in 1979 (Shōwa 54) uncovered grave goods including a replica Naiyōka bun kyō, an imported Shenju mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年" (238 CE), talc beads, iron swords, iron daggers, a bronze bell, and fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}. == Shenju Mirror with the Red Bird 元年 Inscription == [[File:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shenju mirror with paired depictions (National Important Cultural Property)}}{{small|Inscription: "Red Bird 元年" clockwise in the outer border at 1:30–3:00. Displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, other_designation1 as a National Important Cultural Property, is housed at the {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}} in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. It is a 12.5 cm diameter mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年," corresponding to 238 CE, a year in the calendar of the southern Chinese state of {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)}}. In Japan, imported bronze items were regarded as status symbols during the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}, and other imported mirrors have been unearthed in Yamanashi Prefecture. The inscription reflects the geopolitical ties of the era, including Wu's relationship with the {{ill|Gongsun clan|ja|公孫氏}} of the {{ill|Liaodong Peninsula|ja|遼東半島}}, which was destroyed in 238 CE by attacks from {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏}} and {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between {{ill|Yamataikoku|ja|邪馬台国}} and Wei, exemplified by Queen {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == other_designation == === National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}}, housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal Designations === * other_designation ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)}} * {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}} * {{ill|Akura Takatsuka Kofun|ja|安倉高塚古墳}} [[Category:Kofuns in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ichikawa Misato]] Pottery fragments and more | Historic_sites = Designated Municipal other_designation: "Kitsunezuka Kofun" (Ichikawa Misato Town) | Cultural_properties = Shenju mirror (National Important Cultural Property) | notes = }} '''Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (鳥居原狐塚古墳), also known as '''Toriibara Kofun''', is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Ōtsuka, {{ill|Ichikawa Misato|ja|市川三郷町}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}. The tumulus is round in type and has been other_designation1 as a Municipal other_designation under the name "Kitsunezuka Kofun." The Shenju mirror unearthed at the site has been other_designation1 as a National Important Cultural Property. The kofun is notable for the discovery of a Shenju mirror bearing an inscription dating to the first year of the Red Bird era (238 CE). == Overview == The kofun is situated in the southern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}}, at the northeastern edge of Ichikawa Misato Town. It stands on the terminal ridge of the Sone Hills at an elevation of approximately 320 meters. The Sone Hills region is known for its cultural interactions between the {{ill|Tōkai Region|ja|東海地方}} during the Yayoi period and the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}} during the Kofun period, with other Kofuns such as the Ōtsuka Kofun and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} distributed nearby. Although the mound has suffered significant destruction due to land clearing, investigations in 1894 (Meiji 27) revealed a stone chamber. Further studies, including one by Yoshio Nishi in the early Shōwa period, confirmed a pit-style stone chamber constructed from neatly piled split stones, with traces of vermilion pigment on the interior. The absence of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was noted. Excavations in 1979 (Shōwa 54) uncovered grave goods including a replica Naiyōka bun kyō, an imported Shenju mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年" (238 CE), talc beads, iron swords, iron daggers, a bronze bell, and fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}. == Shenju Mirror with the Red Bird 元年 Inscription == [[File:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shenju mirror with paired depictions (National Important Cultural Property)}}{{small|Inscription: "Red Bird 元年" clockwise in the outer border at 1:30–3:00. Displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, other_designation1 as a National Important Cultural Property, is housed at the {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}} in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. It is a 12.5 cm diameter mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年," corresponding to 238 CE, a year in the calendar of the southern Chinese state of {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)}}. In Japan, imported bronze items were regarded as status symbols during the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}, and other imported mirrors have been unearthed in Yamanashi Prefecture. The inscription reflects the geopolitical ties of the era, including Wu's relationship with the {{ill|Gongsun clan|ja|公孫氏}} of the {{ill|Liaodong Peninsula|ja|遼東半島}}, which was destroyed in 238 CE by attacks from {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏}} and {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between {{ill|Yamataikoku|ja|邪馬台国}} and Wei, exemplified by Queen {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == other_designation == === National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}}, housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal Designations === * other_designation ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)}} * {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}} * {{ill|Akura Takatsuka Kofun|ja|安倉高塚古墳}} [[Category:Kofuns in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ichikawa Misato]] The absence of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was noted. Excavations in 1979 (Shōwa 54) uncovered grave goods including a replica Naiyōka bun kyō, an imported Shenju mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年" (238 CE), talc beads, iron swords, iron daggers, a bronze bell, and fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}. == Shenju Mirror with the Red Bird 元年 Inscription == [[File:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shenju mirror with paired depictions (National Important Cultural Property)}}{{small|Inscription: "Red Bird 元年" clockwise in the outer border at 1:30–3:00. Displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, other_designation1 as a National Important Cultural Property, is housed at the {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}} in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. It is a 12.5 cm diameter mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年," corresponding to 238 CE, a year in the calendar of the southern Chinese state of {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)}}. In Japan, imported bronze items were regarded as status symbols during the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}, and other imported mirrors have been unearthed in Yamanashi Prefecture. The inscription reflects the geopolitical ties of the era, including Wu's relationship with the {{ill|Gongsun clan|ja|公孫氏}} of the {{ill|Liaodong Peninsula|ja|遼東半島}}, which was destroyed in 238 CE by attacks from {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏}} and {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between {{ill|Yamataikoku|ja|邪馬台国}} and Wei, exemplified by Queen {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == other_designation == === National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}}, housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal Designations === * other_designation ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Pottery fragments and more | Historic_sites = Designated Municipal other_designation: "Kitsunezuka Kofun" (Ichikawa Misato Town) | Cultural_properties = Shenju mirror (National Important Cultural Property) | notes = }} '''Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (鳥居原狐塚古墳), also known as '''Toriibara Kofun''', is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Ōtsuka, {{ill|Ichikawa Misato|ja|市川三郷町}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}. The tumulus is round in type and has been other_designation1 as a Municipal other_designation under the name "Kitsunezuka Kofun." The Shenju mirror unearthed at the site has been other_designation1 as a National Important Cultural Property. The kofun is notable for the discovery of a Shenju mirror bearing an inscription dating to the first year of the Red Bird era (238 CE). == Overview == The kofun is situated in the southern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}}, at the northeastern edge of Ichikawa Misato Town. It stands on the terminal ridge of the Sone Hills at an elevation of approximately 320 meters. The Sone Hills region is known for its cultural interactions between the {{ill|Tōkai Region|ja|東海地方}} during the Yayoi period and the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}} during the Kofun period, with other Kofuns such as the Ōtsuka Kofun and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} distributed nearby. Although the mound has suffered significant destruction due to land clearing, investigations in 1894 (Meiji 27) revealed a stone chamber. Further studies, including one by Yoshio Nishi in the early Shōwa period, confirmed a pit-style stone chamber constructed from neatly piled split stones, with traces of vermilion pigment on the interior. The absence of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was noted. Excavations in 1979 (Shōwa 54) uncovered grave goods including a replica Naiyōka bun kyō, an imported Shenju mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年" (238 CE), talc beads, iron swords, iron daggers, a bronze bell, and fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}. == Shenju Mirror with the Red Bird 元年 Inscription == [[File:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shenju mirror with paired depictions (National Important Cultural Property)}}{{small|Inscription: "Red Bird 元年" clockwise in the outer border at 1:30–3:00. Displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, other_designation1 as a National Important Cultural Property, is housed at the {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}} in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. It is a 12.5 cm diameter mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年," corresponding to 238 CE, a year in the calendar of the southern Chinese state of {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)}}. In Japan, imported bronze items were regarded as status symbols during the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}, and other imported mirrors have been unearthed in Yamanashi Prefecture. The inscription reflects the geopolitical ties of the era, including Wu's relationship with the {{ill|Gongsun clan|ja|公孫氏}} of the {{ill|Liaodong Peninsula|ja|遼東半島}}, which was destroyed in 238 CE by attacks from {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏}} and {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between {{ill|Yamataikoku|ja|邪馬台国}} and Wei, exemplified by Queen {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == other_designation == === National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}}, housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal Designations === * other_designation ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)}} * {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}} * {{ill|Akura Takatsuka Kofun|ja|安倉高塚古墳}} [[Category:Kofuns in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ichikawa Misato]] The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == other_designation == === National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}}, housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal Designations === * other_designation ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Pottery fragments and more | Historic_sites = Designated Municipal other_designation: "Kitsunezuka Kofun" (Ichikawa Misato Town) | Cultural_properties = Shenju mirror (National Important Cultural Property) | notes = }} '''Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (鳥居原狐塚古墳), also known as '''Toriibara Kofun''', is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Ōtsuka, {{ill|Ichikawa Misato|ja|市川三郷町}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}. The tumulus is round in type and has been other_designation1 as a Municipal other_designation under the name "Kitsunezuka Kofun." The Shenju mirror unearthed at the site has been other_designation1 as a National Important Cultural Property. The kofun is notable for the discovery of a Shenju mirror bearing an inscription dating to the first year of the Red Bird era (238 CE). == Overview == The kofun is situated in the southern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}}, at the northeastern edge of Ichikawa Misato Town. It stands on the terminal ridge of the Sone Hills at an elevation of approximately 320 meters. The Sone Hills region is known for its cultural interactions between the {{ill|Tōkai Region|ja|東海地方}} during the Yayoi period and the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}} during the Kofun period, with other Kofuns such as the Ōtsuka Kofun and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} distributed nearby. Although the mound has suffered significant destruction due to land clearing, investigations in 1894 (Meiji 27) revealed a stone chamber. Further studies, including one by Yoshio Nishi in the early Shōwa period, confirmed a pit-style stone chamber constructed from neatly piled split stones, with traces of vermilion pigment on the interior. The absence of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was noted. Excavations in 1979 (Shōwa 54) uncovered grave goods including a replica Naiyōka bun kyō, an imported Shenju mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年" (238 CE), talc beads, iron swords, iron daggers, a bronze bell, and fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}. == Shenju Mirror with the Red Bird 元年 Inscription == [[File:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shenju mirror with paired depictions (National Important Cultural Property)}}{{small|Inscription: "Red Bird 元年" clockwise in the outer border at 1:30–3:00. Displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, other_designation1 as a National Important Cultural Property, is housed at the {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}} in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. It is a 12.5 cm diameter mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年," corresponding to 238 CE, a year in the calendar of the southern Chinese state of {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)}}. In Japan, imported bronze items were regarded as status symbols during the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}, and other imported mirrors have been unearthed in Yamanashi Prefecture. The inscription reflects the geopolitical ties of the era, including Wu's relationship with the {{ill|Gongsun clan|ja|公孫氏}} of the {{ill|Liaodong Peninsula|ja|遼東半島}}, which was destroyed in 238 CE by attacks from {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏}} and {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between {{ill|Yamataikoku|ja|邪馬台国}} and Wei, exemplified by Queen {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == other_designation == === National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}}, housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal Designations === * other_designation ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)}} * {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}} * {{ill|Akura Takatsuka Kofun|ja|安倉高塚古墳}} [[Category:Kofuns in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ichikawa Misato]] Pottery fragments and more | Historic_sites = Designated Municipal other_designation: "Kitsunezuka Kofun" (Ichikawa Misato Town) | Cultural_properties = Shenju mirror (National Important designation1) | notes = }} '''Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (鳥居原狐塚古墳), also known as '''Toriibara Kofun''', is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Ōtsuka, {{ill|Ichikawa Misato|ja|市川三郷町}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}. The tumulus is round in type and has been other_designation1 as a Municipal other_designation under the name "Kitsunezuka Kofun." The Shenju mirror unearthed at the site has been other_designation1 as a National Important designation1. The kofun is notable for the discovery of a Shenju mirror bearing an inscription dating to the first year of the Red Bird era (238 CE). == Overview == The kofun is situated in the southern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}}, at the northeastern edge of Ichikawa Misato Town. It stands on the terminal ridge of the Sone Hills at an elevation of approximately 320 meters. The Sone Hills region is known for its cultural interactions between the {{ill|Tōkai Region|ja|東海地方}} during the Yayoi period and the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}} during the Kofun period, with other Kofuns such as the Ōtsuka Kofun and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} distributed nearby. Although the mound has suffered significant destruction due to land clearing, investigations in 1894 (Meiji 27) revealed a stone chamber. Further studies, including one by Yoshio Nishi in the early Shōwa period, confirmed a pit-style stone chamber constructed from neatly piled split stones, with traces of vermilion pigment on the interior. The absence of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was noted. Excavations in 1979 (Shōwa 54) uncovered grave goods including a replica Naiyōka bun kyō, an imported Shenju mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年" (238 CE), talc beads, iron swords, iron daggers, a bronze bell, and fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}. == Shenju Mirror with the Red Bird 元年 Inscription == [[File:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shenju mirror with paired depictions (National Important designation1)}}{{small|Inscription: "Red Bird 元年" clockwise in the outer border at 1:30–3:00. Displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, other_designation1 as a National Important designation1, is housed at the {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}} in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. It is a 12.5 cm diameter mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年," corresponding to 238 CE, a year in the calendar of the southern Chinese state of {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)}}. In Japan, imported bronze items were regarded as status symbols during the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}, and other imported mirrors have been unearthed in Yamanashi Prefecture. The inscription reflects the geopolitical ties of the era, including Wu's relationship with the {{ill|Gongsun clan|ja|公孫氏}} of the {{ill|Liaodong Peninsula|ja|遼東半島}}, which was destroyed in 238 CE by attacks from {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏}} and {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between {{ill|Yamataikoku|ja|邪馬台国}} and Wei, exemplified by Queen {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == other_designation == === National [[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}}, housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal Designations === * other_designation ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)}} * {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}} * {{ill|Akura Takatsuka Kofun|ja|安倉高塚古墳}} [[Category:Kofuns in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ichikawa Misato]] Pottery fragments and more | Historic_sites = Designated Municipal other_designation: "Kitsunezuka Kofun" (Ichikawa Misato Town) | Cultural_properties = Shenju mirror (National Important designation1) | notes = }} '''Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (鳥居原狐塚古墳), also known as '''Toriibara Kofun''', is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Ōtsuka, {{ill|Ichikawa Misato|ja|市川三郷町}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}. The tumulus is round in type and has been other_designation1 as a Municipal other_designation under the name "Kitsunezuka Kofun." The Shenju mirror unearthed at the site has been other_designation1 as a National Important designation1. The kofun is notable for the discovery of a Shenju mirror bearing an inscription dating to the first year of the Red Bird era (238 CE). == Overview == The kofun is situated in the southern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}}, at the northeastern edge of Ichikawa Misato Town. It stands on the terminal ridge of the Sone Hills at an elevation of approximately 320 meters. The Sone Hills region is known for its cultural interactions between the {{ill|Tōkai Region|ja|東海地方}} during the Yayoi period and the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}} during the Kofun period, with other Kofuns such as the Ōtsuka Kofun and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} distributed nearby. Although the mound has suffered significant destruction due to land clearing, investigations in 1894 (Meiji 27) revealed a stone chamber. Further studies, including one by Yoshio Nishi in the early Shōwa period, confirmed a pit-style stone chamber constructed from neatly piled split stones, with traces of vermilion pigment on the interior. The absence of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was noted. Excavations in 1979 (Shōwa 54) uncovered grave goods including a replica Naiyōka bun kyō, an imported Shenju mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年" (238 CE), talc beads, iron swords, iron daggers, a bronze bell, and fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}. == Shenju Mirror with the Red Bird 元年 Inscription == [[File:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shenju mirror with paired depictions (National Important designation1)}}{{small|Inscription: "Red Bird 元年" clockwise in the outer border at 1:30–3:00. Displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, other_designation1 as a National Important designation1, is housed at the {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}} in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. It is a 12.5 cm diameter mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年," corresponding to 238 CE, a year in the calendar of the southern Chinese state of {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)}}. In Japan, imported bronze items were regarded as status symbols during the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}, and other imported mirrors have been unearthed in Yamanashi Prefecture. The inscription reflects the geopolitical ties of the era, including Wu's relationship with the {{ill|Gongsun clan|ja|公孫氏}} of the {{ill|Liaodong Peninsula|ja|遼東半島}}, which was destroyed in 238 CE by attacks from {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏}} and {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between {{ill|Yamataikoku|ja|邪馬台国}} and Wei, exemplified by Queen {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == other_designation == === National [[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}}, housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal designation1s === * other_designation ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)}} * {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}} * {{ill|Akura Takatsuka Kofun|ja|安倉高塚古墳}} [[Category:Kofuns in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ichikawa Misato]] Pottery fragments and more | Historic_sites = Designated Municipal other_designation: "Kitsunezuka Kofun" (Ichikawa Misato Town) | Cultural_properties = Shenju mirror (National Important designation1) | notes = }} '''Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (鳥居原狐塚古墳), also known as '''Toriibara Kofun''', is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Ōtsuka, {{ill|Ichikawa Misato|ja|市川三郷町}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}. The tumulus is round in type and has been other_designation1 as a Municipal other_designation under the name "Kitsunezuka Kofun." The Shenju mirror unearthed at the site has been other_designation1 as a National Important designation1. The kofun is notable for the discovery of a Shenju mirror bearing an inscription dating to the first year of the Red Bird era (238 CE). == Overview == The kofun is situated in the southern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}}, at the northeastern edge of Ichikawa Misato Town. It stands on the terminal ridge of the Sone Hills at an elevation of approximately 320 meters. The Sone Hills region is known for its cultural interactions between the {{ill|Tōkai Region|ja|東海地方}} during the Yayoi period and the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}} during the Kofun period, with other Kofuns such as the Ōtsuka Kofun and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} distributed nearby. Although the mound has suffered significant destruction due to land clearing, investigations in 1894 (Meiji 27) revealed a stone chamber. Further studies, including one by Yoshio Nishi in the early Shōwa period, confirmed a pit-style stone chamber constructed from neatly piled split stones, with traces of vermilion pigment on the interior. The absence of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was noted. Excavations in 1979 (Shōwa 54) uncovered grave goods including a replica Naiyōka bun kyō, an imported Shenju mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年" (238 CE), talc beads, iron swords, iron daggers, a bronze bell, and fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}. == Shenju Mirror with the Red Bird 元年 Inscription == [[File:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shenju mirror with paired depictions (National Important designation1)}}{{small|Inscription: "Red Bird 元年" clockwise in the outer border at 1:30–3:00. Displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, other_designation1 as a National Important designation1, is housed at the {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}} in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. It is a 12.5 cm diameter mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年," corresponding to 238 CE, a year in the calendar of the southern Chinese state of {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)}}. In Japan, imported bronze items were regarded as status symbols during the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}, and other imported mirrors have been unearthed in Yamanashi Prefecture. The inscription reflects the geopolitical ties of the era, including Wu's relationship with the {{ill|Gongsun clan|ja|公孫氏}} of the {{ill|Liaodong Peninsula|ja|遼東半島}}, which was destroyed in 238 CE by attacks from {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏}} and {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between {{ill|Yamataikoku|ja|邪馬台国}} and Wei, exemplified by Queen {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == other_designation == === National [[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}}, housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal designation1s === * other_designation ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)}} * {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}} * {{ill|Akura Takatsuka Kofun|ja|安倉高塚古墳}} [[Category:Kofuns in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ichikawa Misato]] Pottery fragments and more | Historic_sites = Designated Municipal Historic Site: "Kitsunezuka Kofun" (Ichikawa Misato Town) | Cultural_properties = Shenju mirror (National Important designation1) | notes = }} '''Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (鳥居原狐塚古墳), also known as '''Toriibara Kofun''', is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Ōtsuka, {{ill|Ichikawa Misato|ja|市川三郷町}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}. The tumulus is round in type and has been Historic Site1 as a Municipal Historic Site under the name "Kitsunezuka Kofun." The Shenju mirror unearthed at the site has been Historic Site1 as a National Important designation1. The kofun is notable for the discovery of a Shenju mirror bearing an inscription dating to the first year of the Red Bird era (238 CE). == Overview == The kofun is situated in the southern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}}, at the northeastern edge of Ichikawa Misato Town. It stands on the terminal ridge of the Sone Hills at an elevation of approximately 320 meters. The Sone Hills region is known for its cultural interactions between the {{ill|Tōkai Region|ja|東海地方}} during the Yayoi period and the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}} during the Kofun period, with other Kofuns such as the Ōtsuka Kofun and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} distributed nearby. Although the mound has suffered significant destruction due to land clearing, investigations in 1894 (Meiji 27) revealed a stone chamber. Further studies, including one by Yoshio Nishi in the early Shōwa period, confirmed a pit-style stone chamber constructed from neatly piled split stones, with traces of vermilion pigment on the interior. The absence of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was noted. Excavations in 1979 (Shōwa 54) uncovered grave goods including a replica Naiyōka bun kyō, an imported Shenju mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年" (238 CE), talc beads, iron swords, iron daggers, a bronze bell, and fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}. == Shenju Mirror with the Red Bird 元年 Inscription == [[File:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shenju mirror with paired depictions (National Important designation1)}}{{small|Inscription: "Red Bird 元年" clockwise in the outer border at 1:30–3:00. Displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, Historic Site1 as a National Important designation1, is housed at the {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}} in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. It is a 12.5 cm diameter mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年," corresponding to 238 CE, a year in the calendar of the southern Chinese state of {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)}}. In Japan, imported bronze items were regarded as status symbols during the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}, and other imported mirrors have been unearthed in Yamanashi Prefecture. The inscription reflects the geopolitical ties of the era, including Wu's relationship with the {{ill|Gongsun clan|ja|公孫氏}} of the {{ill|Liaodong Peninsula|ja|遼東半島}}, which was destroyed in 238 CE by attacks from {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏}} and {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between {{ill|Yamataikoku|ja|邪馬台国}} and Wei, exemplified by Queen {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == Historic Site == === National [[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}}, housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal designation1s === * Historic Site ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)}} * {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}} * {{ill|Akura Takatsuka Kofun|ja|安倉高塚古墳}} [[Category:Kofuns in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ichikawa Misato]] Pottery fragments and more | Historic_sites = Designated Municipal Historic Site: "Kitsunezuka Kofun" (Ichikawa Misato Town) | Cultural_properties = Shenju mirror (National Important designation1) | notes = }} '''Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (鳥居原狐塚古墳), also known as '''Toriibara Kofun''', is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Ōtsuka, {{ill|Ichikawa Misato|ja|市川三郷町}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}. The tumulus is round in type and has been Historic Site1 as a Municipal Historic Site under the name "Kitsunezuka Kofun." The Shenju mirror unearthed at the site has been Historic Site1 as a National Important designation1. The kofun is notable for the discovery of a Shenju mirror bearing an inscription dating to the first year of the Red Bird era (238 CE). == Overview == The kofun is situated in the southern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}}, at the northeastern edge of Ichikawa Misato Town. It stands on the terminal ridge of the Sone Hills at an elevation of approximately 320 meters. The Sone Hills region is known for its cultural interactions between the {{ill|Tōkai Region|ja|東海地方}} during the Yayoi period and the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}} during the Kofun period, with other Kofuns such as the Ōtsuka Kofun and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} distributed nearby. Although the mound has suffered significant destruction due to land clearing, investigations in 1894 (Meiji 27) revealed a stone chamber. Further studies, including one by Yoshio Nishi in the early Shōwa period, confirmed a pit-style stone chamber constructed from neatly piled split stones, with traces of vermilion pigment on the interior. The absence of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was noted. Excavations in 1979 (Shōwa 54) uncovered grave goods including a replica Naiyōka bun kyō, an imported Shenju mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年" (238 CE), talc beads, iron swords, iron daggers, a bronze bell, and fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}. == Shenju Mirror with the Red Bird 元年 Inscription == [[File:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shenju mirror with paired depictions (National Important designation1)}}{{small|Inscription: "Red Bird 元年" clockwise in the outer border at 1:30–3:00. Displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, Historic Site1 as a National Important designation1, is housed at the {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}} in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. It is a 12.5 cm diameter mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年," corresponding to 238 CE, a year in the calendar of the southern Chinese state of {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)}}. In Japan, imported bronze items were regarded as status symbols during the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}, and other imported mirrors have been unearthed in Yamanashi Prefecture. The inscription reflects the geopolitical ties of the era, including Wu's relationship with the {{ill|Gongsun clan|ja|公孫氏}} of the {{ill|Liaodong Peninsula|ja|遼東半島}}, which was destroyed in 238 CE by attacks from {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏}} and {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between {{ill|Yamataikoku|ja|邪馬台国}} and Wei, exemplified by Queen {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == Historic Site == === National [[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}}, housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal designation1s === * Historic Site ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)}} * {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}} * {{ill|Akura Takatsuka Kofun|ja|安倉高塚古墳}} [[Category:Kofuns in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ichikawa Misato]] Height: 3 meters | built = | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} | Interred = | burial goods = {{ill|Shenju mirror|ja|神獣鏡}} with an inscription of "Red Bird" 元年 (238 CE)

Pottery fragments and more | Historic_sites = Designated Municipal Historic Site: "Kitsunezuka Kofun" (Ichikawa Misato Town) | Cultural_properties = Shenju mirror (National Important designation1) | notes = }} '''Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (鳥居原狐塚古墳), also known as '''Toriibara Kofun''', is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in Ōtsuka, {{ill|Ichikawa Misato|ja|市川三郷町}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}. The tumulus is round in type and has been Historic Site1 as a Municipal Historic Site under the name "Kitsunezuka Kofun." The Shenju mirror unearthed at the site has been Historic Site1 as a National Important designation1. The kofun is notable for the discovery of a Shenju mirror bearing an inscription dating to the first year of the Red Bird era (238 CE). == Overview == The kofun is situated in the southern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}}, at the northeastern edge of Ichikawa Misato Town. It stands on the terminal ridge of the Sone Hills at an elevation of approximately 320 meters. The Sone Hills region is known for its cultural interactions between the {{ill|Tōkai Region|ja|東海地方}} during the Yayoi period and the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}} during the Kofun period, with other Kofuns such as the Ōtsuka Kofun and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} distributed nearby. Although the mound has suffered significant destruction due to land clearing, investigations in 1894 (Meiji 27) revealed a stone chamber. Further studies, including one by Yoshio Nishi in the early Shōwa period, confirmed a pit-style stone chamber constructed from neatly piled split stones, with traces of vermilion pigment on the interior. The absence of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was noted. Excavations in 1979 (Shōwa 54) uncovered grave goods including a replica Naiyōka bun kyō, an imported Shenju mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年" (238 CE), talc beads, iron swords, iron daggers, a bronze bell, and fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}. == Shenju Mirror with the Red Bird 元年 Inscription == [[File:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shenju mirror with paired depictions (National Important designation1)}}{{small|Inscription: "Red Bird 元年" clockwise in the outer border at 1:30–3:00. Displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}.}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, Historic Site1 as a National Important designation1, is housed at the {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}} in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. It is a 12.5 cm diameter mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年," corresponding to 238 CE, a year in the calendar of the southern Chinese state of {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)}}. In Japan, imported bronze items were regarded as status symbols during the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}, and other imported mirrors have been unearthed in Yamanashi Prefecture. The inscription reflects the geopolitical ties of the era, including Wu's relationship with the {{ill|Gongsun clan|ja|公孫氏}} of the {{ill|Liaodong Peninsula|ja|遼東半島}}, which was destroyed in 238 CE by attacks from {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏}} and {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗}}. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between {{ill|Yamataikoku|ja|邪馬台国}} and Wei, exemplified by Queen {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼}}'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == Historic Site == === National [[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社}}, housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal designation1s === * Historic Site ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)}} * {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵}} * {{ill|Akura Takatsuka Kofun|ja|安倉高塚古墳}} of Japan]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofuns in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ichikawa Misato]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:[[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Q--q]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]] [[ja:鳥居原狐塚古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun}}

Pottery fragments and more | Historic_sites = Designated Municipal Historic Site: "Kitsunezuka Kofun" (Ichikawa Misato Town) | Cultural_properties = Shenju mirror (National Important designation1) | notes = }} '''Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (鳥居原狐塚古墳), also known as '''Toriibara Kofun''', is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in Ōtsuka, {{ill|Ichikawa Misato|ja|市川三郷町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft}}. The tumulus is round in type and has been Historic Site1 as a Municipal Historic Site under the name "Kitsunezuka Kofun." The Shenju mirror unearthed at the site has been Historic Site1 as a National Important designation1. The kofun is notable for the discovery of a Shenju mirror bearing an inscription dating to the first year of the Red Bird era (238 CE). == Overview == The kofun is situated in the southern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=draft}}, at the northeastern edge of Ichikawa Misato Town. It stands on the terminal ridge of the Sone Hills at an elevation of approximately 320 meters. The Sone Hills region is known for its cultural interactions between the {{ill|Tōkai Region|ja|東海地方|12=draft}} during the Yayoi period and the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内|12=draft}} during the Kofun period, with other Kofuns such as the Ōtsuka Kofun and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|伊勢塚古墳|12=draft}} distributed nearby. Although the mound has suffered significant destruction due to land clearing, investigations in 1894 (Meiji 27) revealed a stone chamber. Further studies, including one by Yoshio Nishi in the early Shōwa period, confirmed a pit-style stone chamber constructed from neatly piled split stones, with traces of vermilion pigment on the interior. The absence of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} was noted. Excavations in 1979 (Shōwa 54) uncovered grave goods including a replica Naiyōka bun kyō, an imported Shenju mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年" (238 CE), talc beads, iron swords, iron daggers, a bronze bell, and fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}. == Shenju Mirror with the Red Bird 元年 Inscription == [[File:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shenju mirror with paired depictions (National Important designation1)}}{{small|Inscription: "Red Bird 元年" clockwise in the outer border at 1:30–3:00. Displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}}.}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, Historic Site1 as a National Important designation1, is housed at the {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社|12=draft}} in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}}. It is a 12.5 cm diameter mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年," corresponding to 238 CE, a year in the calendar of the southern Chinese state of {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)|12=draft}}. In Japan, imported bronze items were regarded as status symbols during the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft}}, and other imported mirrors have been unearthed in Yamanashi Prefecture. The inscription reflects the geopolitical ties of the era, including Wu's relationship with the {{ill|Gongsun clan|ja|公孫氏|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Liaodong Peninsula|ja|遼東半島|12=draft}}, which was destroyed in 238 CE by attacks from {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏|12=draft}} and {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗|12=draft}}. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志|12=draft}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between {{ill|Yamataikoku|ja|邪馬台国|12=draft}} and Wei, exemplified by Queen {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼|12=draft}}'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == Historic Site == === National [[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社|12=draft}}, housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}}. Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal designation1s === * Historic Site ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵|12=draft}} * {{ill|Akura Takatsuka Kofun|ja|安倉高塚古墳|12=draft}} of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofuns in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ichikawa Misato]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:[[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Q--q]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]] [[ja:鳥居原狐塚古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun}}

Pottery fragments and more | Historic_sites = Designated Municipal Historic Site: "Kitsunezuka Kofun" (Ichikawa Misato Town) | Cultural_properties = Shenju mirror (National Important designation1) | notes = }} '''Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (鳥居原狐塚古墳), also known as '''Toriibara Kofun''', is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in Ōtsuka, {{ill|Ichikawa Misato|ja|市川三郷町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft}}. The tumulus is round in type and has been Historic Site1 as a Municipal Historic Site under the name "Kitsunezuka Kofun." The Shenju mirror unearthed at the site has been Historic Site1 as a National Important designation1. The kofun is notable for the discovery of a Shenju mirror bearing an inscription dating to the first year of the Red Bird era (238 CE). == Overview == The kofun is situated in the southern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=draft}}, at the northeastern edge of Ichikawa Misato Town. It stands on the terminal ridge of the Sone Hills at an elevation of approximately 320 meters. The Sone Hills region is known for its cultural interactions between the {{ill|Tōkai Region|ja|東海地方|12=draft}} during the Yayoi period and the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内|12=draft}} during the Kofun period, with other Kofuns such as the Ōtsuka Kofun and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|伊勢塚古墳|12=draft}} distributed nearby. Although the mound has suffered significant destruction due to land clearing, investigations in 1894 (Meiji 27) revealed a stone chamber. Further studies, including one by Yoshio Nishi in the early Shōwa period, confirmed a pit-style stone chamber constructed from neatly piled split stones, with traces of vermilion pigment on the interior. The absence of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} was noted. Excavations in 1979 (Shōwa 54) uncovered grave goods including a replica Naiyōka bun kyō, an imported Shenju mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年" (238 CE), talc beads, iron swords, iron daggers, a bronze bell, and fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}. == Shenju Mirror with the Red Bird 元年 Inscription == [[File:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shenju mirror with paired depictions (National Important designation1)}}{{small|Inscription: "Red Bird 元年" clockwise in the outer border at 1:30–3:00. Displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}}.}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, Historic Site1 as a National Important designation1, is housed at the {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社|12=draft}} in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}}. It is a 12.5 cm diameter mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年," corresponding to 238 CE, a year in the calendar of the southern Chinese state of {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)|12=draft}}. In Japan, imported bronze items were regarded as status symbols during the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft}}, and other imported mirrors have been unearthed in Yamanashi Prefecture. The inscription reflects the geopolitical ties of the era, including Wu's relationship with the {{ill|Gongsun clan|ja|公孫氏|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Liaodong Peninsula|ja|遼東半島|12=draft}}, which was destroyed in 238 CE by attacks from {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏|12=draft}} and {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗|12=draft}}. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志|12=draft}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between {{ill|Yamataikoku|ja|邪馬台国|12=draft}} and Wei, exemplified by Queen {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼|12=draft}}'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == Historic Site == === National [[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社|12=draft}}, housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}}. Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal designation1s === * Historic Site ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵|12=draft}} * {{ill|Akura Takatsuka Kofun|ja|安倉高塚古墳|12=draft}} of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofuns in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ichikawa Misato]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:[[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Q--q]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]] [[ja:鳥居原狐塚古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}}

Pottery fragments and more | Historic_sites = Designated Municipal Historic Site: "Kitsunezuka Kofun" (Ichikawa Misato Town) | Cultural_properties = Shenju mirror (National Important designation1) | notes = }} '''Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (鳥居原狐塚古墳), also known as '''Toriibara Kofun''', is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in Ōtsuka, {{ill|Ichikawa Misato|ja|市川三郷町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft}}. The tumulus is round in type and has been Historic Site1 as a Municipal Historic Site under the name "Kitsunezuka Kofun." The Shenju mirror unearthed at the site has been Historic Site1 as a National Important designation1. The kofun is notable for the discovery of a Shenju mirror bearing an inscription dating to the first year of the Red Bird era (238 CE). == Overview == The kofun is situated in the southern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=draft}}, at the northeastern edge of Ichikawa Misato Town. It stands on the terminal ridge of the Sone Hills at an elevation of approximately 320 meters. The Sone Hills region is known for its cultural interactions between the {{ill|Tōkai Region|ja|東海地方|12=draft}} during the Yayoi period and the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内|12=draft}} during the Kofun period, with other Kofuns such as the Ōtsuka Kofun and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|伊勢塚古墳|12=draft}} distributed nearby. Although the mound has suffered significant destruction due to land clearing, investigations in 1894 (Meiji 27) revealed a stone chamber. Further studies, including one by Yoshio Nishi in the early Shōwa period, confirmed a pit-style stone chamber constructed from neatly piled split stones, with traces of vermilion pigment on the interior. The absence of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} was noted. Excavations in 1979 (Shōwa 54) uncovered grave goods including a replica Naiyōka bun kyō, an imported Shenju mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年" (238 CE), talc beads, iron swords, iron daggers, a bronze bell, and fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}. == Shenju Mirror with the Red Bird 元年 Inscription == [[File:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shenju mirror with paired depictions (National Important designation1)}}{{small|Inscription: "Red Bird 元年" clockwise in the outer border at 1:30–3:00. Displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}}.}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, Historic Site1 as a National Important designation1, is housed at the {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社|12=draft}} in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}}. It is a 12.5 cm diameter mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年," corresponding to 238 CE, a year in the calendar of the southern Chinese state of {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)|12=draft}}. In Japan, imported bronze items were regarded as status symbols during the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft}}, and other imported mirrors have been unearthed in Yamanashi Prefecture. The inscription reflects the geopolitical ties of the era, including Wu's relationship with the {{ill|Gongsun clan|ja|公孫氏|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Liaodong Peninsula|ja|遼東半島|12=draft}}, which was destroyed in 238 CE by attacks from {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏|12=draft}} and {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗|12=draft}}. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志|12=draft}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between {{ill|Yamataikoku|ja|邪馬台国|12=draft}} and Wei, exemplified by Queen {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼|12=draft}}'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == Historic Site == === National [[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社|12=draft}}, housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}}. Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal designation1s === * Historic Site ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵|12=draft}} * {{ill|Akura Takatsuka Kofun|ja|安倉高塚古墳|12=draft}} of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofuns in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ichikawa Misato]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:[[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]] [[ja:鳥居原狐塚古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofuns | name = Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun (鳥居原狐塚古墳) | image = [[File:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]

Height: 3 meters | built = | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} | Interred = | burial goods = {{ill|Shenju mirror|ja|神獣鏡|12=draft}} with an inscription of "Red Bird" 元年 (238 CE)
Replica {{ill|Naiyōka bun kyō|ja|内行花文鏡|12=draft}}

Pottery fragments and more | Historic_sites = Designated Municipal Historic Site: "Kitsunezuka Kofun" (Ichikawa Misato Town) | Cultural_properties = Shenju mirror (National Important designation1) | notes = }} '''Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (鳥居原狐塚古墳), also known as '''Toriibara Kofun''', is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in Ōtsuka, {{ill|Ichikawa Misato|ja|市川三郷町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft}}. The tumulus is round in type and has been Historic Site1 as a Municipal Historic Site under the name "Kitsunezuka Kofun." The Shenju mirror unearthed at the site has been Historic Site1 as a National Important designation1. The kofun is notable for the discovery of a Shenju mirror bearing an inscription dating to the first year of the Red Bird era (238 CE). == Overview == The kofun is situated in the southern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=draft}}, at the northeastern edge of Ichikawa Misato Town. It stands on the terminal ridge of the Sone Hills at an elevation of approximately 320 meters. The Sone Hills region is known for its cultural interactions between the {{ill|Tōkai Region|ja|東海地方|12=draft}} during the Yayoi period and the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内|12=draft}} during the Kofun period, with other Kofuns such as the Ōtsuka Kofun and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|伊勢塚古墳|12=draft}} distributed nearby. Although the mound has suffered significant destruction due to land clearing, investigations in 1894 (Meiji 27) revealed a stone chamber. Further studies, including one by Yoshio Nishi in the early Shōwa period, confirmed a pit-style stone chamber constructed from neatly piled split stones, with traces of vermilion pigment on the interior. The absence of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} was noted. Excavations in 1979 (Shōwa 54) uncovered grave goods including a replica Naiyōka bun kyō, an imported Shenju mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年" (238 CE), talc beads, iron swords, iron daggers, a bronze bell, and fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}. == Shenju Mirror with the Red Bird 元年 Inscription == [[File:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shenju mirror with paired depictions (National Important designation1)}}{{small|Inscription: "Red Bird 元年" clockwise in the outer border at 1:30–3:00. Displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}}.}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, Historic Site1 as a National Important designation1, is housed at the {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社|12=draft}} in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}}. It is a 12.5 cm diameter mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年," corresponding to 238 CE, a year in the calendar of the southern Chinese state of {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)|12=draft}}. In Japan, imported bronze items were regarded as status symbols during the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft}}, and other imported mirrors have been unearthed in Yamanashi Prefecture. The inscription reflects the geopolitical ties of the era, including Wu's relationship with the {{ill|Gongsun clan|ja|公孫氏|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Liaodong Peninsula|ja|遼東半島|12=draft}}, which was destroyed in 238 CE by attacks from {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏|12=draft}} and {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗|12=draft}}. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志|12=draft}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between {{ill|Yamataikoku|ja|邪馬台国|12=draft}} and Wei, exemplified by Queen {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼|12=draft}}'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == Historic Site == === National [[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社|12=draft}}, housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}}. Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal designation1s === * Historic Site ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵|12=draft}} * {{ill|Akura Takatsuka Kofun|ja|安倉高塚古墳|12=draft}} of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofuns in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ichikawa Misato]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:[[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]] [[ja:鳥居原狐塚古墳]] {{citation needed|Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofuns | name = Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun (鳥居原狐塚古墳) | image = [[File:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Intypeation board near the site | alternate_name = Toriibara Kofun | location = {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nishiyatsushiro District|ja|西八代郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ichikawa Misato|ja|市川三郷町|12=draft}}, Ōtsuka | coordinates = {{ill|Geographical coordinates|ja|ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|34|33.8|N|138|32|45|E|map_size:10000|12=draft}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | area = Diameter: 18 meters (estimated)
Height: 3 meters | built = | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} | Interred = | burial goods = {{ill|Shenju mirror|ja|神獣鏡|12=draft}} with an inscription of "Red Bird" 元年 (238 CE)
Replica {{ill|Naiyōka bun kyō|ja|内行花文鏡|12=draft}}
{{ill|Iron sword|ja|鉄刀|12=draft}}
Pottery fragments and more | Historic_sites = Designated Municipal Historic Site: "Kitsunezuka Kofun" (Ichikawa Misato Town) | Cultural_properties = Shenju mirror (National Important designation1) | notes = }} '''Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (鳥居原狐塚古墳), also known as '''Toriibara Kofun''', is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in Ōtsuka, {{ill|Ichikawa Misato|ja|市川三郷町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft}}. The tumulus is round in type and has been Historic Site1 as a Municipal Historic Site under the name "Kitsunezuka Kofun." The Shenju mirror unearthed at the site has been Historic Site1 as a National Important designation1. The kofun is notable for the discovery of a Shenju mirror bearing an inscription dating to the first year of the Red Bird era (238 CE). == Overview == The kofun is situated in the southern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=draft}}, at the northeastern edge of Ichikawa Misato Town. It stands on the terminal ridge of the Sone Hills at an elevation of approximately 320 meters. The Sone Hills region is known for its cultural interactions between the {{ill|Tōkai Region|ja|東海地方|12=draft}} during the Yayoi period and the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内|12=draft}} during the Kofun period, with other Kofuns such as the Ōtsuka Kofun and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|伊勢塚古墳|12=draft}} distributed nearby. Although the mound has suffered significant destruction due to land clearing, investigations in 1894 (Meiji 27) revealed a stone chamber. Further studies, including one by Yoshio Nishi in the early Shōwa period, confirmed a pit-style stone chamber constructed from neatly piled split stones, with traces of vermilion pigment on the interior. The absence of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} was noted. Excavations in 1979 (Shōwa 54) uncovered grave goods including a replica Naiyōka bun kyō, an imported Shenju mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年" (238 CE), talc beads, iron swords, iron daggers, a bronze bell, and fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}. == Shenju Mirror with the Red Bird 元年 Inscription == [[File:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shenju mirror with paired depictions (National Important designation1)}}{{small|Inscription: "Red Bird 元年" clockwise in the outer border at 1:30–3:00. Displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}}.}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, Historic Site1 as a National Important designation1, is housed at the {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社|12=draft}} in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}}. It is a 12.5 cm diameter mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年," corresponding to 238 CE, a year in the calendar of the southern Chinese state of {{ill|Wu (Three Kingdoms)|ja|呉 (三国)|12=draft}}. In Japan, imported bronze items were regarded as status symbols during the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft}}, and other imported mirrors have been unearthed in Yamanashi Prefecture. The inscription reflects the geopolitical ties of the era, including Wu's relationship with the {{ill|Gongsun clan|ja|公孫氏|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Liaodong Peninsula|ja|遼東半島|12=draft}}, which was destroyed in 238 CE by attacks from {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏|12=draft}} and {{ill|Goguryeo|ja|高句麗|12=draft}}. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志|12=draft}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between {{ill|Yamataikoku|ja|邪馬台国|12=draft}} and Wei, exemplified by Queen {{ill|Himiko|ja|卑弥呼|12=draft}}'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == Historic Site == === National [[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|ja|一宮浅間神社|12=draft}}, housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}}. Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal designation1s === * Historic Site ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵|12=draft}} * {{ill|Akura Takatsuka Kofun|ja|安倉高塚古墳|12=draft}} of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofuns in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ichikawa Misato]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:[[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun]] [[ja:鳥居原狐塚古墳]] {{citation needed|Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}
