The absence of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] was noted. Excavations in 1979 (Shōwa 54) uncovered grave goods including a replica Naiyōka bun kyō, an imported Shenju mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年" (238 CE), talc beads, iron swords, iron daggers, a bronze bell, and fragments of [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]. == Shenju Mirror with the Red Bird 元年 Inscription == [[File:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shenju mirror with paired depictions (National Important designation1)}}{{small|Inscription: "Red Bird 元年" clockwise in the outer border at 1:30–3:00. Displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]].}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, Historic Site1 as a National Important designation1, is housed at the [[:en:Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine]] in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. It is a 12.5 cm diameter mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年," corresponding to 238 CE, a year in the calendar of the southern Chinese state of [[:en:Eastern Wu|Wu (Three Kingdoms)]]. In Japan, imported bronze items were regarded as status symbols during the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], and other imported mirrors have been unearthed in Yamanashi Prefecture. The inscription reflects the geopolitical ties of the era, including Wu's relationship with the [[:en:Gongsun|Gongsun clan]] of the [[:en:Liaodong Peninsula|Liaodong Peninsula]], which was destroyed in 238 CE by attacks from {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wei (Three Kingdoms)|1=Cao Wei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124257886}} and [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]]. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Records of the Three Kingdoms|1=Three Kingdoms|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354746}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between [[:en:Yamatai|Yamataikoku]] and Wei, exemplified by Queen [[:en:Himiko|Himiko]]'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == Historic Site == === National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by [[:en:Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine]], housed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal designation1s === * Historic Site ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|1=Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433997}} * {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Hills|qq=}} * {{ill|Akura Takatsuka Kofun|ja|安倉高塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akura Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鳥居原狐塚古墳|version=103618861|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:鳥居原狐塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ichikawa-Misato Town]] {{wikidata link|Q11674888}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11674888}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Pottery fragments and more | Historic_sites = Designated Municipal Historic Site: "Kitsunezuka Kofun" (Ichikawa Misato Town) | Cultural_properties = Shenju mirror (National Important designation1) | notes = }} '''Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (鳥居原狐塚古墳), also known as '''Toriibara Kofun''', is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Ōtsuka, [[:en:Ichikawamisato|Ichikawa Misato]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]. The tumulus is round in type and has been Historic Site1 as a Municipal Historic Site under the name "Kitsunezuka Kofun." The Shenju mirror unearthed at the site has been Historic Site1 as a National Important designation1. The kofun is notable for the discovery of a Shenju mirror bearing an inscription dating to the first year of the Red Bird era (238 CE). == Overview == The kofun is situated in the southern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōfu Basin|qq=}}, at the northeastern edge of Ichikawa Misato Town. It stands on the terminal ridge of the Sone Hills at an elevation of approximately 320 meters. The Sone Hills region is known for its cultural interactions between the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai Region]] during the Yayoi period and the [[:en:Kinai|Kinai region]] during the Kofun period, with other Kofuns such as the Ōtsuka Kofun and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|伊勢塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|1=Isezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q113498764}} distributed nearby. Although the mound has suffered significant destruction due to land clearing, investigations in 1894 (Meiji 27) revealed a stone chamber. Further studies, including one by Yoshio Nishi in the early Shōwa period, confirmed a pit-style stone chamber constructed from neatly piled split stones, with traces of vermilion pigment on the interior. The absence of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] was noted. Excavations in 1979 (Shōwa 54) uncovered grave goods including a replica Naiyōka bun kyō, an imported Shenju mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年" (238 CE), talc beads, iron swords, iron daggers, a bronze bell, and fragments of [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]. == Shenju Mirror with the Red Bird 元年 Inscription == [[File:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shenju mirror with paired depictions (National Important designation1)}}{{small|Inscription: "Red Bird 元年" clockwise in the outer border at 1:30–3:00. Displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]].}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, Historic Site1 as a National Important designation1, is housed at the [[:en:Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine]] in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. It is a 12.5 cm diameter mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年," corresponding to 238 CE, a year in the calendar of the southern Chinese state of [[:en:Eastern Wu|Wu (Three Kingdoms)]]. In Japan, imported bronze items were regarded as status symbols during the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], and other imported mirrors have been unearthed in Yamanashi Prefecture. The inscription reflects the geopolitical ties of the era, including Wu's relationship with the [[:en:Gongsun|Gongsun clan]] of the [[:en:Liaodong Peninsula|Liaodong Peninsula]], which was destroyed in 238 CE by attacks from {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wei (Three Kingdoms)|1=Cao Wei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124257886}} and [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]]. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Records of the Three Kingdoms|1=Three Kingdoms|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354746}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between [[:en:Yamatai|Yamataikoku]] and Wei, exemplified by Queen [[:en:Himiko|Himiko]]'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. Height: 3 meters | built = | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Interred = | burial goods = [[:en:Shinju-kyo|Shenju mirror]] with an inscription of "Red Bird" 元年 (238 CE)

{{ill|Iron sword|ja|鉄刀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iron sword|1=iron sword|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48750050}}
Pottery fragments and more | Historic_sites = Designated Municipal Historic Site: "Kitsunezuka Kofun" (Ichikawa Misato Town) | Cultural_properties = Shenju mirror (National Important designation1) | notes = }} '''Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (鳥居原狐塚古墳), also known as '''Toriibara Kofun''', is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Ōtsuka, [[:en:Ichikawamisato|Ichikawa Misato]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]. The tumulus is round in type and has been Historic Site1 as a Municipal Historic Site under the name "Kitsunezuka Kofun." The Shenju mirror unearthed at the site has been Historic Site1 as a National Important designation1. The kofun is notable for the discovery of a Shenju mirror bearing an inscription dating to the first year of the Red Bird era (238 CE). == Overview == The kofun is situated in the southern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōfu Basin|qq=}}, at the northeastern edge of Ichikawa Misato Town. It stands on the terminal ridge of the Sone Hills at an elevation of approximately 320 meters. The Sone Hills region is known for its cultural interactions between the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai Region]] during the Yayoi period and the [[:en:Kinai|Kinai region]] during the Kofun period, with other Kofuns such as the Ōtsuka Kofun and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|伊勢塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|1=Isezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q113498764}} distributed nearby. Although the mound has suffered significant destruction due to land clearing, investigations in 1894 (Meiji 27) revealed a stone chamber. Further studies, including one by Yoshio Nishi in the early Shōwa period, confirmed a pit-style stone chamber constructed from neatly piled split stones, with traces of vermilion pigment on the interior. The absence of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] was noted. Excavations in 1979 (Shōwa 54) uncovered grave goods including a replica Naiyōka bun kyō, an imported Shenju mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年" (238 CE), talc beads, iron swords, iron daggers, a bronze bell, and fragments of [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]. == Shenju Mirror with the Red Bird 元年 Inscription == [[File:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shenju mirror with paired depictions (National Important designation1)}}{{small|Inscription: "Red Bird 元年" clockwise in the outer border at 1:30–3:00. Displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]].}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, Historic Site1 as a National Important designation1, is housed at the [[:en:Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine]] in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. It is a 12.5 cm diameter mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年," corresponding to 238 CE, a year in the calendar of the southern Chinese state of [[:en:Eastern Wu|Wu (Three Kingdoms)]]. In Japan, imported bronze items were regarded as status symbols during the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], and other imported mirrors have been unearthed in Yamanashi Prefecture. The inscription reflects the geopolitical ties of the era, including Wu's relationship with the [[:en:Gongsun|Gongsun clan]] of the [[:en:Liaodong Peninsula|Liaodong Peninsula]], which was destroyed in 238 CE by attacks from {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wei (Three Kingdoms)|1=Cao Wei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124257886}} and [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]]. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Records of the Three Kingdoms|1=Three Kingdoms|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354746}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between [[:en:Yamatai|Yamataikoku]] and Wei, exemplified by Queen [[:en:Himiko|Himiko]]'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == Historic Site == === National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by [[:en:Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine]], housed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal designation1s === * Historic Site ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|1=Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433997}} * {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Hills|qq=}} * {{ill|Akura Takatsuka Kofun|ja|安倉高塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akura Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鳥居原狐塚古墳|version=103618861|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:鳥居原狐塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ichikawa-Misato Town]] {{wikidata link|Q11674888}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11674888}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal designation1s === * Historic Site ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|1=Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433997}} * {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Hills|qq=}} * {{ill|Akura Takatsuka Kofun|ja|安倉高塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akura Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鳥居原狐塚古墳|version=103618861|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:鳥居原狐塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ichikawa-Misato Town]] {{wikidata link|Q11674888}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11674888}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]]

Pottery fragments and more | Historic_sites = Designated Municipal Historic Site: "Kitsunezuka Kofun" (Ichikawa Misato Town) | Cultural_properties = Shenju mirror (National Important designation1) | notes = }} '''Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (鳥居原狐塚古墳), also known as '''Toriibara Kofun''', is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Ōtsuka, [[:en:Ichikawamisato|Ichikawa Misato]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]. The tumulus is round in type and has been Historic Site1 as a Municipal Historic Site under the name "Kitsunezuka Kofun." The Shenju mirror unearthed at the site has been Historic Site1 as a National Important designation1. The kofun is notable for the discovery of a Shenju mirror bearing an inscription dating to the first year of the Red Bird era (238 CE). == Overview == The kofun is situated in the southern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōfu Basin|qq=}}, at the northeastern edge of Ichikawa Misato Town. It stands on the terminal ridge of the Sone Hills at an elevation of approximately 320 meters. The Sone Hills region is known for its cultural interactions between the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai Region]] during the Yayoi period and the [[:en:Kinai|Kinai region]] during the Kofun period, with other Kofuns such as the Ōtsuka Kofun and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|伊勢塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|1=Isezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q113498764}} distributed nearby. Although the mound has suffered significant destruction due to land clearing, investigations in 1894 (Meiji 27) revealed a stone chamber. Further studies, including one by Yoshio Nishi in the early Shōwa period, confirmed a pit-style stone chamber constructed from neatly piled split stones, with traces of vermilion pigment on the interior. The absence of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] was noted. Excavations in 1979 (Shōwa 54) uncovered grave goods including a replica Naiyōka bun kyō, an imported Shenju mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年" (238 CE), talc beads, iron swords, iron daggers, a bronze bell, and fragments of [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]. == Shenju Mirror with the Red Bird 元年 Inscription == [[File:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shenju mirror with paired depictions (National Important designation1)}}{{small|Inscription: "Red Bird 元年" clockwise in the outer border at 1:30–3:00. Displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]].}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, Historic Site1 as a National Important designation1, is housed at the [[:en:Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine]] in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. It is a 12.5 cm diameter mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年," corresponding to 238 CE, a year in the calendar of the southern Chinese state of [[:en:Eastern Wu|Wu (Three Kingdoms)]]. In Japan, imported bronze items were regarded as status symbols during the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], and other imported mirrors have been unearthed in Yamanashi Prefecture. The inscription reflects the geopolitical ties of the era, including Wu's relationship with the [[:en:Gongsun|Gongsun clan]] of the [[:en:Liaodong Peninsula|Liaodong Peninsula]], which was destroyed in 238 CE by attacks from {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wei (Three Kingdoms)|1=Cao Wei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124257886}} and [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]]. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Records of the Three Kingdoms|1=Three Kingdoms|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354746}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between [[:en:Yamatai|Yamataikoku]] and Wei, exemplified by Queen [[:en:Himiko|Himiko]]'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == Historic Site == === National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by [[:en:Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine]], housed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal designation1s === * Historic Site ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|1=Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433997}} * {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Hills|qq=}} * {{ill|Akura Takatsuka Kofun|ja|安倉高塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akura Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鳥居原狐塚古墳|version=103618861|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:鳥居原狐塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ichikawa-Misato Town]] {{wikidata link|Q11674888}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11674888}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

Pottery fragments and more | Historic_sites = Designated Municipal Historic Site: "Kitsunezuka Kofun" (Ichikawa Misato Town) | Cultural_properties = Shenju mirror (National Important designation1) | notes = }} '''Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (鳥居原狐塚古墳), also known as '''Toriibara Kofun''', is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Ōtsuka, [[:en:Ichikawamisato|Ichikawa Misato]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]. The tumulus is round in type and has been Historic Site1 as a Municipal Historic Site under the name "Kitsunezuka Kofun." The Shenju mirror unearthed at the site has been Historic Site1 as a National Important designation1. The kofun is notable for the discovery of a Shenju mirror bearing an inscription dating to the first year of the Red Bird era (238 CE). == Overview == The kofun is situated in the southern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōfu Basin|qq=}}, at the northeastern edge of Ichikawa Misato Town. It stands on the terminal ridge of the Sone Hills at an elevation of approximately 320 meters. The Sone Hills region is known for its cultural interactions between the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai Region]] during the Yayoi period and the [[:en:Kinai|Kinai region]] during the Kofun period, with other Kofuns such as the Ōtsuka Kofun and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|伊勢塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|1=Isezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q113498764}} distributed nearby. Although the mound has suffered significant destruction due to land clearing, investigations in 1894 (Meiji 27) revealed a stone chamber. Further studies, including one by Yoshio Nishi in the early Shōwa period, confirmed a pit-style stone chamber constructed from neatly piled split stones, with traces of vermilion pigment on the interior. The absence of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] was noted. Excavations in 1979 (Shōwa 54) uncovered grave goods including a replica Naiyōka bun kyō, an imported Shenju mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年" (238 CE), talc beads, iron swords, iron daggers, a bronze bell, and fragments of [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]. == Shenju Mirror with the Red Bird 元年 Inscription == [[File:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shenju mirror with paired depictions (National Important designation1)}}{{small|Inscription: "Red Bird 元年" clockwise in the outer border at 1:30–3:00. Displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]].}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, Historic Site1 as a National Important designation1, is housed at the [[:en:Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine]] in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. It is a 12.5 cm diameter mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年," corresponding to 238 CE, a year in the calendar of the southern Chinese state of [[:en:Eastern Wu|Wu (Three Kingdoms)]]. In Japan, imported bronze items were regarded as status symbols during the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], and other imported mirrors have been unearthed in Yamanashi Prefecture. The inscription reflects the geopolitical ties of the era, including Wu's relationship with the [[:en:Gongsun|Gongsun clan]] of the [[:en:Liaodong Peninsula|Liaodong Peninsula]], which was destroyed in 238 CE by attacks from {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wei (Three Kingdoms)|1=Cao Wei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124257886}} and [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]]. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Records of the Three Kingdoms|1=Three Kingdoms|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354746}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between [[:en:Yamatai|Yamataikoku]] and Wei, exemplified by Queen [[:en:Himiko|Himiko]]'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == Historic Site == === National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by [[:en:Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine]], housed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal designation1s === * Historic Site ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|1=Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433997}} * {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Hills|qq=}} * {{ill|Akura Takatsuka Kofun|ja|安倉高塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akura Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鳥居原狐塚古墳|version=103618861|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:鳥居原狐塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ichikawa-Misato Town]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q11674888}}

Pottery fragments and more | Historic_sites = Designated Municipal Historic Site: "Kitsunezuka Kofun" (Ichikawa Misato Town) | Cultural_properties = Shenju mirror (National Important designation1) | notes = }} '''Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (鳥居原狐塚古墳), also known as '''Toriibara Kofun''', is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Ōtsuka, [[:en:Ichikawamisato|Ichikawa Misato]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]. The tumulus is round in type and has been Historic Site1 as a Municipal Historic Site under the name "Kitsunezuka Kofun." The Shenju mirror unearthed at the site has been Historic Site1 as a National Important designation1. The kofun is notable for the discovery of a Shenju mirror bearing an inscription dating to the first year of the Red Bird era (238 CE). == Overview == The kofun is situated in the southern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōfu Basin|qq=}}, at the northeastern edge of Ichikawa Misato Town. It stands on the terminal ridge of the Sone Hills at an elevation of approximately 320 meters. The Sone Hills region is known for its cultural interactions between the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai Region]] during the Yayoi period and the [[:en:Kinai|Kinai region]] during the Kofun period, with other Kofuns such as the Ōtsuka Kofun and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|伊勢塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|1=Isezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q113498764}} distributed nearby. Although the mound has suffered significant destruction due to land clearing, investigations in 1894 (Meiji 27) revealed a stone chamber. Further studies, including one by Yoshio Nishi in the early Shōwa period, confirmed a pit-style stone chamber constructed from neatly piled split stones, with traces of vermilion pigment on the interior. The absence of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] was noted. Excavations in 1979 (Shōwa 54) uncovered grave goods including a replica Naiyōka bun kyō, an imported Shenju mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年" (238 CE), talc beads, iron swords, iron daggers, a bronze bell, and fragments of [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]. == Shenju Mirror with the Red Bird 元年 Inscription == [[File:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shenju mirror with paired depictions (National Important designation1)}}{{small|Inscription: "Red Bird 元年" clockwise in the outer border at 1:30–3:00. Displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]].}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, Historic Site1 as a National Important designation1, is housed at the [[:en:Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine]] in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. It is a 12.5 cm diameter mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年," corresponding to 238 CE, a year in the calendar of the southern Chinese state of [[:en:Eastern Wu|Wu (Three Kingdoms)]]. In Japan, imported bronze items were regarded as status symbols during the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], and other imported mirrors have been unearthed in Yamanashi Prefecture. The inscription reflects the geopolitical ties of the era, including Wu's relationship with the [[:en:Gongsun|Gongsun clan]] of the [[:en:Liaodong Peninsula|Liaodong Peninsula]], which was destroyed in 238 CE by attacks from {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wei (Three Kingdoms)|1=Cao Wei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124257886}} and [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]]. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Records of the Three Kingdoms|1=Three Kingdoms|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354746}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between [[:en:Yamatai|Yamataikoku]] and Wei, exemplified by Queen [[:en:Himiko|Himiko]]'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == Historic Site == === National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by [[:en:Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine]], housed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal designation1s === * Historic Site ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|1=Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433997}} * {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Hills|qq=}} * {{ill|Akura Takatsuka Kofun|ja|安倉高塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akura Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鳥居原狐塚古墳|version=103618861|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:鳥居原狐塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ichikawa-Misato Town]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11674888}}

According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Records of the Three Kingdoms|1=Three Kingdoms|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354746}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between [[:en:Yamatai|Yamataikoku]] and Wei, exemplified by Queen [[:en:Himiko|Himiko]]'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == Historic Site == === National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by [[:en:Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine]], housed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal designation1s === * Historic Site ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|1=Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433997}} * {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Hills|qq=}} * {{ill|Akura Takatsuka Kofun|ja|安倉高塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akura Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鳥居原狐塚古墳|version=103618861|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:鳥居原狐塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ichikawa-Misato Town]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11674888}} [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

Pottery fragments and more | Historic_sites = Designated Municipal Historic Site: "Kitsunezuka Kofun" (Ichikawa Misato Town) | Cultural_properties = Shenju mirror (National Important designation1) | notes = }} '''Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (鳥居原狐塚古墳), also known as '''Toriibara Kofun''', is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Ōtsuka, [[:en:Ichikawamisato|Ichikawa Misato]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]. The tumulus is round in type and has been Historic Site1 as a Municipal Historic Site under the name "Kitsunezuka Kofun." The Shenju mirror unearthed at the site has been Historic Site1 as a National Important designation1. The kofun is notable for the discovery of a Shenju mirror bearing an inscription dating to the first year of the Red Bird era (238 CE). == Overview == The kofun is situated in the southern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōfu Basin|qq=}}, at the northeastern edge of Ichikawa Misato Town. It stands on the terminal ridge of the Sone Hills at an elevation of approximately 320 meters. The Sone Hills region is known for its cultural interactions between the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai Region]] during the Yayoi period and the [[:en:Kinai|Kinai region]] during the Kofun period, with other Kofuns such as the Ōtsuka Kofun and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|伊勢塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|1=Isezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q113498764}} distributed nearby. Although the mound has suffered significant destruction due to land clearing, investigations in 1894 (Meiji 27) revealed a stone chamber. Further studies, including one by Yoshio Nishi in the early Shōwa period, confirmed a pit-style stone chamber constructed from neatly piled split stones, with traces of vermilion pigment on the interior. The absence of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] was noted. Excavations in 1979 (Shōwa 54) uncovered grave goods including a replica Naiyōka bun kyō, an imported Shenju mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年" (238 CE), talc beads, iron swords, iron daggers, a bronze bell, and fragments of [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]. == Shenju Mirror with the Red Bird 元年 Inscription == [[File:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shenju mirror with paired depictions (National Important designation1)}}{{small|Inscription: "Red Bird 元年" clockwise in the outer border at 1:30–3:00. Displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]].}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, Historic Site1 as a National Important designation1, is housed at the [[:en:Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine]] in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. It is a 12.5 cm diameter mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年," corresponding to 238 CE, a year in the calendar of the southern Chinese state of [[:en:Eastern Wu|Wu (Three Kingdoms)]]. In Japan, imported bronze items were regarded as status symbols during the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], and other imported mirrors have been unearthed in Yamanashi Prefecture. The inscription reflects the geopolitical ties of the era, including Wu's relationship with the [[:en:Gongsun|Gongsun clan]] of the [[:en:Liaodong Peninsula|Liaodong Peninsula]], which was destroyed in 238 CE by attacks from {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wei (Three Kingdoms)|1=Cao Wei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124257886}} and [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]]. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Records of the Three Kingdoms|1=Three Kingdoms|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354746}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between [[:en:Yamatai|Yamataikoku]] and Wei, exemplified by Queen [[:en:Himiko|Himiko]]'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == Historic Site == === National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by [[:en:Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine]], housed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal designation1s === * Historic Site ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|1=Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433997}} * {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Hills|qq=}} * {{ill|Akura Takatsuka Kofun|ja|安倉高塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akura Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鳥居原狐塚古墳|version=103618861|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:鳥居原狐塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11674888}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Ichikawa-Misato Town]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

The Shenju mirror unearthed at the site has been Historic Site1 as a National Important designation1. The kofun is notable for the discovery of a Shenju mirror bearing an inscription dating to the first year of the Red Bird era (238 CE). == Overview == The kofun is situated in the southern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōfu Basin|qq=}}, at the northeastern edge of Ichikawa Misato Town. It stands on the terminal ridge of the Sone Hills at an elevation of approximately 320 meters. The Sone Hills region is known for its cultural interactions between the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai Region]] during the Yayoi period and the [[:en:Kinai|Kinai region]] during the Kofun period, with other Kofuns such as the Ōtsuka Kofun and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|伊勢塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|1=Isezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q113498764}} distributed nearby. Although the mound has suffered significant destruction due to land clearing, investigations in 1894 (Meiji 27) revealed a stone chamber. Further studies, including one by Yoshio Nishi in the early Shōwa period, confirmed a pit-style stone chamber constructed from neatly piled split stones, with traces of vermilion pigment on the interior. The absence of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] was noted. Excavations in 1979 (Shōwa 54) uncovered grave goods including a replica Naiyōka bun kyō, an imported Shenju mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年" (238 CE), talc beads, iron swords, iron daggers, a bronze bell, and fragments of [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]. == Shenju Mirror with the Red Bird 元年 Inscription == [[File:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shenju mirror with paired depictions (National Important designation1)}}{{small|Inscription: "Red Bird 元年" clockwise in the outer border at 1:30–3:00. Displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]].}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, Historic Site1 as a National Important designation1, is housed at the [[:en:Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine]] in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. It is a 12.5 cm diameter mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年," corresponding to 238 CE, a year in the calendar of the southern Chinese state of [[:en:Eastern Wu|Wu (Three Kingdoms)]]. In Japan, imported bronze items were regarded as status symbols during the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], and other imported mirrors have been unearthed in Yamanashi Prefecture. The inscription reflects the geopolitical ties of the era, including Wu's relationship with the [[:en:Gongsun|Gongsun clan]] of the [[:en:Liaodong Peninsula|Liaodong Peninsula]], which was destroyed in 238 CE by attacks from {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wei (Three Kingdoms)|1=Cao Wei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124257886}} and [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]]. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Records of the Three Kingdoms|1=Three Kingdoms|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354746}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between [[:en:Yamatai|Yamataikoku]] and Wei, exemplified by Queen [[:en:Himiko|Himiko]]'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == Historic Site == === National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by [[:en:Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine]], housed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal designation1s === * Historic Site ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|1=Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433997}} * {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Hills|qq=}} * {{ill|Akura Takatsuka Kofun|ja|安倉高塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akura Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鳥居原狐塚古墳|version=103618861|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鳥居原狐塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11674888}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Ichikawa-Misato Town]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofuns | name = Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun (鳥居原狐塚古墳) | image = [[File:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]
Intypeation board near the site | alternate_name = Toriibara Kofun | location = [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], [[:en:Nishiyatsushiro District, Yamanashi|Nishiyatsushiro District]], [[:en:Ichikawamisato|Ichikawa Misato]], Ōtsuka | coordinates = {{ill|Geographical coordinates|ja|ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|34|33.8|N|138|32|45|E|map_size:10000|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geographical coordinates|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Diameter: 18 meters (estimated)
Height: 3 meters | built = | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Interred = | burial goods = [[:en:Shinju-kyo|Shenju mirror]] with an inscription of "Red Bird" 元年 (238 CE)
Replica [[:en:Flower mirror|Flower mirror]]
{{ill|Iron sword|ja|鉄刀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iron sword|1=iron sword|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48750050}}
Pottery fragments and more | Historic_sites = Designated Municipal Historic Site: "Kitsunezuka Kofun" (Ichikawa Misato Town) | Cultural_properties = Shenju mirror (National Important designation1) | notes = }} '''Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (鳥居原狐塚古墳), also known as '''Toriibara Kofun''', is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in Ōtsuka, [[:en:Ichikawamisato|Ichikawa Misato]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]. The tumulus is round in type and has been Historic Site1 as a Municipal Historic Site under the name "Kitsunezuka Kofun." The Shenju mirror unearthed at the site has been Historic Site1 as a National Important designation1. The kofun is notable for the discovery of a Shenju mirror bearing an inscription dating to the first year of the Red Bird era (238 CE). == Overview == The kofun is situated in the southern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōfu Basin|qq=}}, at the northeastern edge of Ichikawa Misato Town. It stands on the terminal ridge of the Sone Hills at an elevation of approximately 320 meters. The Sone Hills region is known for its cultural interactions between the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai Region]] during the Yayoi period and the [[:en:Kinai|Kinai region]] during the Kofun period, with other Kofuns such as the Ōtsuka Kofun and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|伊勢塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|1=Isezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q113498764}} distributed nearby. Although the mound has suffered significant destruction due to land clearing, investigations in 1894 (Meiji 27) revealed a stone chamber. Further studies, including one by Yoshio Nishi in the early Shōwa period, confirmed a pit-style stone chamber constructed from neatly piled split stones, with traces of vermilion pigment on the interior. The absence of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] was noted. Excavations in 1979 (Shōwa 54) uncovered grave goods including a replica Naiyōka bun kyō, an imported Shenju mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年" (238 CE), talc beads, iron swords, iron daggers, a bronze bell, and fragments of [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]. == Shenju Mirror with the Red Bird 元年 Inscription == [[File:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shenju mirror with paired depictions (National Important designation1)}}{{small|Inscription: "Red Bird 元年" clockwise in the outer border at 1:30–3:00. Displayed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]].}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, Historic Site1 as a National Important designation1, is housed at the [[:en:Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine]] in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. It is a 12.5 cm diameter mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年," corresponding to 238 CE, a year in the calendar of the southern Chinese state of [[:en:Eastern Wu|Wu (Three Kingdoms)]]. In Japan, imported bronze items were regarded as status symbols during the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], and other imported mirrors have been unearthed in Yamanashi Prefecture. The inscription reflects the geopolitical ties of the era, including Wu's relationship with the [[:en:Gongsun|Gongsun clan]] of the [[:en:Liaodong Peninsula|Liaodong Peninsula]], which was destroyed in 238 CE by attacks from {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wei (Three Kingdoms)|1=Cao Wei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124257886}} and [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]]. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Records of the Three Kingdoms|1=Three Kingdoms|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354746}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between [[:en:Yamatai|Yamataikoku]] and Wei, exemplified by Queen [[:en:Himiko|Himiko]]'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == Historic Site == === National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by [[:en:Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine]], housed at the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal designation1s === * Historic Site ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|1=Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433997}} * {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Hills|qq=}} * {{ill|Akura Takatsuka Kofun|ja|安倉高塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akura Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鳥居原狐塚古墳|version=103618861|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鳥居原狐塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11674888}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Ichikawa-Misato Town]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}}.}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, Historic Site1 as a National Important designation1, is housed at the {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|en|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|WD=Q11352589|lt=Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine}} in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}}. It is a 12.5 cm diameter mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年," corresponding to 238 CE, a year in the calendar of the southern Chinese state of {{ill|Eastern Wu|en|Eastern Wu|WD=Q274488|lt=Wu (Three Kingdoms)}}. In Japan, imported bronze items were regarded as status symbols during the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}}, and other imported mirrors have been unearthed in Yamanashi Prefecture. The inscription reflects the geopolitical ties of the era, including Wu's relationship with the {{ill|Gongsun|en|Gongsun|WD=Q1537169|lt=Gongsun clan}} of the {{ill|Liaodong Peninsula|en|Liaodong Peninsula|WD=Q326917|lt=Liaodong Peninsula}}, which was destroyed in 238 CE by attacks from {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wei (Three Kingdoms)|1=Cao Wei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124257886}} and {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo}}. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Records of the Three Kingdoms|1=Three Kingdoms|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354746}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between {{ill|Yamatai|en|Yamatai|WD=Q844295|lt=Yamataikoku}} and Wei, exemplified by Queen {{ill|Himiko|en|Himiko|WD=Q234451|lt=Himiko}}'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == Historic Site == === National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}} === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|en|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|WD=Q11352589|lt=Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine}}, housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}}. Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal designation1s === * Historic Site ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|1=Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433997}} * {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Hills|qq=}} * {{ill|Akura Takatsuka Kofun|ja|安倉高塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akura Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鳥居原狐塚古墳|version=103618861|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鳥居原狐塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11674888}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Ichikawa-Misato Town]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofuns | name = Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun (鳥居原狐塚古墳) | image = [[File:Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, gaikan.jpg|280px]]

Pottery fragments and more | Historic_sites = Designated Municipal Historic Site: "Kitsunezuka Kofun" (Ichikawa Misato Town) | Cultural_properties = Shenju mirror (National Important designation1) | notes = }} '''Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (鳥居原狐塚古墳), also known as '''Toriibara Kofun''', is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in Ōtsuka, {{ill|Ichikawamisato|en|Ichikawamisato|WD=Q1204453|lt=Ichikawa Misato}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture}}. The tumulus is round in type and has been Historic Site1 as a Municipal Historic Site under the name "Kitsunezuka Kofun." The Shenju mirror unearthed at the site has been Historic Site1 as a National Important designation1. The kofun is notable for the discovery of a Shenju mirror bearing an inscription dating to the first year of the Red Bird era (238 CE). == Overview == The kofun is situated in the southern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōfu Basin|qq=}}, at the northeastern edge of Ichikawa Misato Town. It stands on the terminal ridge of the Sone Hills at an elevation of approximately 320 meters. The Sone Hills region is known for its cultural interactions between the {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|WD=Q398989|lt=Tōkai Region}} during the Yayoi period and the {{ill|Kinai|en|Kinai|WD=Q906708|lt=Kinai region}} during the Kofun period, with other Kofuns such as the Ōtsuka Kofun and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|伊勢塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|1=Isezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q113498764}} distributed nearby. Although the mound has suffered significant destruction due to land clearing, investigations in 1894 (Meiji 27) revealed a stone chamber. Further studies, including one by Yoshio Nishi in the early Shōwa period, confirmed a pit-style stone chamber constructed from neatly piled split stones, with traces of vermilion pigment on the interior. The absence of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} was noted. Excavations in 1979 (Shōwa 54) uncovered grave goods including a replica Naiyōka bun kyō, an imported Shenju mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年" (238 CE), talc beads, iron swords, iron daggers, a bronze bell, and fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}. == Shenju Mirror with the Red Bird 元年 Inscription == [[File:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shenju mirror with paired depictions (National Important designation1)}}{{small|Inscription: "Red Bird 元年" clockwise in the outer border at 1:30–3:00. Displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}}.}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, Historic Site1 as a National Important designation1, is housed at the {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|en|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|WD=Q11352589|lt=Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine}} in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}}. It is a 12.5 cm diameter mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年," corresponding to 238 CE, a year in the calendar of the southern Chinese state of {{ill|Eastern Wu|en|Eastern Wu|WD=Q274488|lt=Wu (Three Kingdoms)}}. In Japan, imported bronze items were regarded as status symbols during the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}}, and other imported mirrors have been unearthed in Yamanashi Prefecture. The inscription reflects the geopolitical ties of the era, including Wu's relationship with the {{ill|Gongsun|en|Gongsun|WD=Q1537169|lt=Gongsun clan}} of the {{ill|Liaodong Peninsula|en|Liaodong Peninsula|WD=Q326917|lt=Liaodong Peninsula}}, which was destroyed in 238 CE by attacks from {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wei (Three Kingdoms)|1=Cao Wei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124257886}} and {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo}}. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Records of the Three Kingdoms|1=Three Kingdoms|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354746}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between {{ill|Yamatai|en|Yamatai|WD=Q844295|lt=Yamataikoku}} and Wei, exemplified by Queen {{ill|Himiko|en|Himiko|WD=Q234451|lt=Himiko}}'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == Historic Site == === National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}} === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|en|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|WD=Q11352589|lt=Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine}}, housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}}. Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal designation1s === * Historic Site ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|1=Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433997}} * {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Hills|qq=}} * {{ill|Akura Takatsuka Kofun|ja|安倉高塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akura Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鳥居原狐塚古墳|version=103618861|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鳥居原狐塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11674888}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Ichikawa-Misato Town]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Height: 3 meters | built = | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | Interred = | burial goods = {{ill|Shinju-kyo|en|Shinju-kyo|WD=Q7497614|lt=Shenju mirror}} with an inscription of "Red Bird" 元年 (238 CE)
Replica [[:en:Flower mirror|Flower mirror]]
{{ill|Iron sword|ja|鉄刀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iron sword|1=iron sword|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48750050}}
Pottery fragments and more | Historic_sites = Designated Municipal Historic Site: "Kitsunezuka Kofun" (Ichikawa Misato Town) | Cultural_properties = Shenju mirror (National Important designation1) | notes = }} '''Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun''' (鳥居原狐塚古墳), also known as '''Toriibara Kofun''', is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in Ōtsuka, {{ill|Ichikawamisato|en|Ichikawamisato|WD=Q1204453|lt=Ichikawa Misato}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture}}. The tumulus is round in type and has been Historic Site1 as a Municipal Historic Site under the name "Kitsunezuka Kofun." The Shenju mirror unearthed at the site has been Historic Site1 as a National Important designation1. The kofun is notable for the discovery of a Shenju mirror bearing an inscription dating to the first year of the Red Bird era (238 CE). == Overview == The kofun is situated in the southern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōfu Basin|qq=}}, at the northeastern edge of Ichikawa Misato Town. It stands on the terminal ridge of the Sone Hills at an elevation of approximately 320 meters. The Sone Hills region is known for its cultural interactions between the {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|WD=Q398989|lt=Tōkai Region}} during the Yayoi period and the {{ill|Kinai|en|Kinai|WD=Q906708|lt=Kinai region}} during the Kofun period, with other Kofuns such as the Ōtsuka Kofun and {{ill|Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|伊勢塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isezuka Kofun (Ichikawa Misato)|1=Isezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q113498764}} distributed nearby. Although the mound has suffered significant destruction due to land clearing, investigations in 1894 (Meiji 27) revealed a stone chamber. Further studies, including one by Yoshio Nishi in the early Shōwa period, confirmed a pit-style stone chamber constructed from neatly piled split stones, with traces of vermilion pigment on the interior. The absence of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} was noted. Excavations in 1979 (Shōwa 54) uncovered grave goods including a replica Naiyōka bun kyō, an imported Shenju mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年" (238 CE), talc beads, iron swords, iron daggers, a bronze bell, and fragments of {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}. == Shenju Mirror with the Red Bird 元年 Inscription == [[File:鳥居原古墳出土 対置式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shenju mirror with paired depictions (National Important designation1)}}{{small|Inscription: "Red Bird 元年" clockwise in the outer border at 1:30–3:00. Displayed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}}.}}]] The Shenju mirror from this kofun, Historic Site1 as a National Important designation1, is housed at the {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|en|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|WD=Q11352589|lt=Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine}} in Ichikawa Misato and is on loan to the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}}. It is a 12.5 cm diameter mirror inscribed with "Red Bird 元年," corresponding to 238 CE, a year in the calendar of the southern Chinese state of {{ill|Eastern Wu|en|Eastern Wu|WD=Q274488|lt=Wu (Three Kingdoms)}}. In Japan, imported bronze items were regarded as status symbols during the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}}, and other imported mirrors have been unearthed in Yamanashi Prefecture. The inscription reflects the geopolitical ties of the era, including Wu's relationship with the {{ill|Gongsun|en|Gongsun|WD=Q1537169|lt=Gongsun clan}} of the {{ill|Liaodong Peninsula|en|Liaodong Peninsula|WD=Q326917|lt=Liaodong Peninsula}}, which was destroyed in 238 CE by attacks from {{ill|Wei (Three Kingdoms)|ja|魏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wei (Three Kingdoms)|1=Cao Wei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124257886}} and {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo}}. According to the {{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|ja|三国志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Records of the Three Kingdoms|1=Three Kingdoms|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354746}}, this period coincides with diplomatic exchanges between {{ill|Yamatai|en|Yamatai|WD=Q844295|lt=Yamataikoku}} and Wei, exemplified by Queen {{ill|Himiko|en|Himiko|WD=Q234451|lt=Himiko}}'s tribute to Wei in 239 CE. The mirror may have been brought to Japan as part of diplomatic exchanges with Wu or through intermediaries like the Gongsun clan. == Historic Site == === National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}} === * Shenju Mirror inscribed "Red Bird 元年 May 25," unearthed at Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun, Yamanashi Prefecture (archaeological artifact) – Owned by {{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|en|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|WD=Q11352589|lt=Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine}}, housed at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}}. Designated on June 6, 1979. === Municipal designation1s === * Historic Site ** Kitsunezuka Kofun – Designated on June 19, 1976. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|ja|大塚古墳群 (市川三郷町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|1=Ōtsuka Kofun Group (Ichikawa Misato)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433997}} * {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hills|ja|曽根丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Hills|qq=}} * {{ill|Akura Takatsuka Kofun|ja|安倉高塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akura Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鳥居原狐塚古墳|version=103618861|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鳥居原狐塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11674888}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Ichikawa-Misato Town]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]
